get.description:
Liveness check endpoint.

Answers only "is the process up and serving HTTP?" — it asserts no
downstream dependency on purpose. A liveness failure tells the
orchestrator to *restart* the container, and a restart can't fix a
slow JWKS endpoint or a transient DB blip, so depending on those
here would cause needless restart storms. The readiness probe
(``/v1/ready``) is the one that gates traffic on real dependencies.

Also acts as the lightweight FE-facing config-passthrough surface for
runtime feature flags whose value the FE has to react to (e.g.
``PADDINGTON_SHARE_LINK_ENABLED`` per DEV-1031 / DEV-1038). Adding
new top-level flags here is fine; if the surface grows beyond a
handful of booleans we can split it into a dedicated
``/v1/config`` endpoint.

Returns:
    Status dict with service health information and FE-visible flags.

Used by the SessionPanel "Delete session" destructive action. The

patch.description:

Body shape: ``{"add": [...], "remove": [...]}``. Both fields are

already present or removing one that isn't is silently accepted —
the call always succeeds with the resulting tag list.

put.description:
Set one schema-driven annotation cell on a session.

The value is validated against the column's type (a 422 on a mismatched
shape). The reserved ``verdict`` cell is the canonical evaluator-accuracy
source — writing it updates accuracy directly. A ``null`` value clears the
cell. Returns 404 for a missing / cross-org session or a column not in the
project's schema.

delete.description:
Clear one schema-driven annotation cell on a session.

Idempotent — clearing an already-empty cell succeeds (204). Clearing the
reserved ``verdict`` cell returns the session to "Not evaluated" for
evaluator accuracy (a NULL verdict value is excluded from the accuracy
denominator). Returns 404 only for a missing / cross-org session or a
column not in the project's schema.

get.description:
Return the project's distinct, sorted CROSS-SURFACE tag vocabulary.

The Tags pickers on BOTH the Sessions table and the flat Scenarios

TrustAI keeps no separate vocabulary table — the source of truth is
the UNION of every session's ``tags`` JSONB array AND every scenario's
(``dataset_rows.tags``) JSONB array scoped to the project, deduped and
sorted. Returns ``{"tags": []}`` for projects with no tagged sessions
or scenarios.

get.description:
List the connected agents a project can sync sessions from.

Powers the Sessions header "Sync now" per-agent dropdown. Returns the
project's connected agents projected to the human-readable
``{id, name, system_name}`` shape, in host order — the FE groups by
``system_name`` (the source system). Same host list the sync iterates.

post.description:
Pull historical conversations from a project's connected agent(s).

DEV-708 (M9-02). Synchronous (the gateway walks AgentCore Memory →
Sessions → Events on the customer's behalf). Returns an aggregate
``{synced, skipped, errors}`` shape — a single misconfigured host
surfaces in ``errors`` rather than aborting the rest of the sync.

The Sessions "Sync now" dropdown sends ``{"host_id": "..."}`` to scope the
sync to exactly one connected agent (single-select). When the body is
omitted (or ``host_id`` is null), every syncable host on the project is
walked — the project-wide behaviour.

Classic Bedrock Agents (and other non-syncable connected agents the user
explicitly targets) produce an entry in ``errors`` with a "Sync not
supported" message rather than a silent no-op — they have no
server-side session-list API.

Run one batch of a checkpoint-driven Bedrock sync.

The FE drives a loop against this endpoint so a long sync issues many
short HTTP calls (each capped server-side) instead of a single
multi-minute request. Each call advances one host one batch, persists
the new sessions, and returns the resume cursor plus per-batch
counters for the progress indicator. The loop continues while
``has_more`` is true; when the current host completes, the response
names the next host so the next call advances directly to it.

Cancelling between calls leaves all already-persisted sessions and
checkpoints intact; resuming starts from the persisted checkpoint
automatically when the FE omits it on the first call.

Add or remove tags on every session matching ``body.selection``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Supports both Mode A (ids list) and Mode B
(filter + excluded_ids) via the ``selection.mode`` discriminator.
Single ``UPDATE`` per request.

Server-streamed CSV export of every session matching ``body.selection``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Uses ``StreamingResponse`` + a chunked async
generator so a million-row export never materialises in either FE
or BE memory. The cursor is closed on consumer abort.

The tenant guard runs here, BEFORE the ``StreamingResponse`` is
constructed — once the 200 + CSV header are on the wire there is no
way to take them back, so the denial has to fire pre-stream.

Materialise Examples from matching Sessions into ``dataset_id``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Batches of 100 inside one logical operation; the
dataset version bumps once at the end (per the M3 PRD bump matrix).

Accept an OTLP HTTP ``ExportTraceServiceRequest`` protobuf payload.

Returns a JSON body shape ``{"accepted_spans": N, "written_sessions":
M}`` — the OTLP spec only requires 200 on success but a JSON body is
cheap and useful for the in-process self-test that follows the dual-
exporter wiring.

The receiver:

1. Decodes the protobuf body. A malformed payload returns 400 with
   the parse error in ``detail``.
2. Groups spans by ``trace_id`` so a single push that batches
   multiple turns produces multiple ``sessions`` rows.
3. Projects each trace through
   :func:`project_openinference_trace` to a ``NormalizedTurn``.
4. Writes via
   :func:`sessions_service.get_or_create_with_id` — idempotent on
   the trace-id-derived session id.

get.description:
Return a paginated, kind-filtered audit log for a dataset.

Powers the dataset version-history viewer (M6-13) and the example
side panel's "Edit history" tab (M6-05). Sorted newest first;
consumers render top-down without re-sorting. See
:func:`src.domain.datasets.service.list_events` for filter
semantics and pagination contract.

List a project's scenarios, paginated and scoped to the caller's organization.

Returns one entry per scenario in the project — each carrying its
goal, attached personas (id and human name), tags, the person who
created it, how many times it has been run, and how many
conversations a single run fans out into. A project that belongs to
another organization yields no scenarios, never another
organization's data.

Return the built-in catalog of recommended adversarial scenarios.

A read-only, tenant-independent starting library — one curated probe
per OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications (2026) failure mode, each
paired with the recommended evaluators that score it well. Adoption
(copying a scenario into a project) happens frontend-side through the
existing dataset-rows write path; this endpoint only serves the
catalog.

Declared above ``GET /{scenario_id}`` so the literal ``recommended``
segment is matched before the parameterized scenario lookup and never
shadowed by it.

Return one scenario's read shape, scoped to the caller's organization.

A scenario that does not exist — or one that belongs to another
organization — is reported as not found rather than revealing that
it exists for someone else.

patch.description:
Persist an inline Scenario edit (currently: the Name) and return the
refreshed read shape.

The project-scoped, tenant-scoped write side of the flat Scenario
facade. Addressed by the scenario id directly so the ``/scenarios``
table's inline Name edit lands without an O(datasets) probe to find
the owning dataset. A scenario that does not exist — or one that
belongs to another organization — is reported as not found, never
revealing that it exists for someone else.

delete.description:
Delete one scenario, addressed by id, scoped to the caller's organization.

The delete side of the flat ``/scenarios`` facade. Resolves the
owning storage parent internally so the caller never needs it. The
block-if-evaluated policy is enforced server-side: a scenario that
evaluation history references is refused with a 409
(``DatasetRowInUseError``) carrying human-readable copy, rather than
being deleted and orphaning that history. Sessions the scenario
produced survive (their link is cleared, their frozen snapshot kept).

A scenario that does not exist — or belongs to another organization
— is reported as not found, never revealing that it exists for
someone else.

get.description:
Return the pre-delete impact signals for one scenario.

Read by the delete confirmation UX (the fly-in and the bulk action
bar) before it commits, so it can block on the BLOCKER and warn on
the WARNING without re-deriving the rule:

* ``evaluation_run_count`` — the BLOCKER. While non-zero, ``DELETE``
  refuses with a 409 (evaluation history would be orphaned).
  ``can_delete`` mirrors this gate.
* ``affected_session_count`` — the WARNING. Those sessions survive
  the delete (their link is cleared, their snapshot kept); the count
  only tells the user how many runs will be unlinked.

A scenario that does not exist — or belongs to another organization
— is reported as not found, never revealing that it exists for
someone else.

post.description:
Create an evaluation run from the unified launcher's payload.

The single create surface behind the EvaluationLauncher modal. One
typed body routes to one of four branches inferred from
``source`` / ``scope`` / ``run_only``:

* **scenarios bulk** — simulate each scenario then grade it with its
  inherited evaluators (``simulate_at_eval_time`` on; ``evaluator_ids``
  must be absent — they are inherited).
* **scenarios run-only** — simulate without grading (no evaluators
  required; the suppress-grading intent threads to the worker).
* **sessions bulk** — replay the dataset rows materialised from the
  chosen sessions and grade them with the picked LLM judges
  (``simulate_at_eval_time`` off; at least one judge required).
* **one-off** — an ephemeral, unnamed single-item evaluation run IN
  PLACE. Mints NO run row (no auto-named orphan in the Evaluations
  list); the result is homed directly on the graded session
  (``run_id IS NULL``). A sessions one-off grades the chosen session's
  stored transcript; a scenarios one-off simulates the scenario into a
  "From eval" session, then grades it (so it needs a connected agent).

Returns the pending run (or, for a one-off, a transient pending
acknowledgement carrying no persisted run). Validation failures surface
as calm 4xx errors the launcher renders inline.

get.description:
Group evaluation runs by ``agent_version_id`` for the project (DEV-668).

One row per distinct agent version (legacy unpinned runs collapse
into a single ``agent_version_id=None`` bucket). Each row carries
the run count, summed totals, and the average pass rate across
runs in the bucket — enough for the M4 dashboard to render a
cross-version comparison table without a second round-trip.

Project-wide aggregate stats for the Evaluations list subtitle.

Backs the list page header line
"<N> total runs · <N> in last 7 days · <X> avg pass rate".

* ``total_runs`` counts every run in the project, any status.
* ``runs_last_7d`` counts runs *completed* in the last 7 days.
* ``avg_pass_rate`` is the row-weighted mean of
  ``evaluation_results.passed_all`` over all result rows of
  completed runs ("% of examples that passed") — ``null`` when no
  completed run has been scored.

Kept separate from the paginated list endpoint so the stats stay
project-wide and page-independent: re-paginating the list never
re-runs this aggregate, and a page-scoped denominator (which would
understate the numbers on page 2+) is structurally impossible.

An empty project returns ``total_runs=0``, ``runs_last_7d=0``,
``avg_pass_rate=null`` — a clean success response, not an error.

post.description:
Preview how a dataset's rows split into evaluation cohorts.

Consumed by the Evaluation New Run form's "What this run will
evaluate" cards: as the user picks a dataset / agent version /
evaluators, the form posts the three values here and renders the
returned counts live.

The split is decided by each row's value in the dataset's pass/fail
annotation column — the column whose ``type == 'pass_fail'``. A
*pass* verdict makes the row ``agent_testable``; a *fail* verdict
makes it ``currently_failing``; either way it is ``evaluator_testable``.
A row with no verdict is ``unusable``.

**No pass/fail column contract:** when the chosen dataset has no
pass/fail column at all, no row can carry a verdict, so the
response reports every row as ``unusable`` and both testable
cohorts as ``0``. This is a documented success response, not an
error — the form simply shows a zero gradable cohort.

``agent_version_id`` is validated for existence (a stale picker
value yields a 404) but does not currently gate row inclusion;
``evaluator_ids`` is accepted for a stable request contract and is
not consulted. A missing ``dataset_id`` or ``agent_version_id``
returns 404.

patch.description:
Rename an evaluation run.

Backs the Run Detail header's inline-editable title — committing
the edited title sends ``{"name": "<new label>"}`` here so a user
can label a finished run ("baseline", "post-prompt-tweak"). Only
the run's ``name`` is touched; status, counters, and timestamps are
left untouched.

Returns the updated run. An unknown ``run_id`` yields a 404; an
empty / whitespace-only / oversized ``name`` is rejected as a 422
by :class:`RenameEvaluationRunInput` before the service runs.

delete.description:
Soft-delete an evaluation run.

Backs the Evaluations list row menu's "Delete run" action (and the
Run Detail header's equivalent). The run is archived, not removed:
it disappears from every list / aggregate / detail surface, while
its results stay in the database so audit history and from-eval
Session links remain resolvable.

Allowed in any run state. Deleting an in-flight (``pending`` /
``running``) run also requests cancellation so the evaluation
worker stops scheduling new dataset rows at its next row boundary.

Returns 204 on success. An unknown, cross-tenant, or
already-deleted ``run_id`` yields a 404 — after a successful
delete, the run reads as gone everywhere, including from a repeat
DELETE.

get.description:
Resolve the launcher pre-fill seed for a Re-run.

Re-run opens the EvaluationLauncher modal in-place, pre-seeded from
this server-resolved seed — it never round-trips a dataset id. The
seed carries the original run's source, the items it targeted, the
evaluators it graded with, its pinned agent, the run-only flag, and a
suggested name for the re-run.

Tenant-scoped to the session org: a cross-org run yields a 404 (never
a 403), so existence never leaks across the tenant boundary.

post.description:
Cancel an in-flight evaluation run.

Operator-initiated: while a run is ``pending`` or ``running`` the
Run Detail header shows a "Cancel run" button that calls this
endpoint. The run transitions to the terminal ``canceled`` state —
either immediately (when the run was still ``pending`` and no
worker had picked it up) or once the evaluation worker drains its
in-flight dataset rows and observes the cancel flag.

Returns the updated run. A run that has already reached a terminal
state (``completed`` / ``failed`` / ``canceled``) yields a 409 —
the run exists but there is nothing left to cancel. An unknown
``run_id`` yields a 404.

get.description:
Stream an evaluation run's results as a downloadable CSV file.

Server-streamed via ``StreamingResponse`` + an async generator so a
large result set never materialises in BE memory. Each row in the
CSV represents one (result x evaluator) score — a result scored by
N evaluators produces N CSV rows.

The ``attachment`` disposition pins the download filename to the
run's name. Mirrors the Datasets surface's streamed CSV export
(``GET /v1/datasets/{id}/export.csv``).

Return one result rendered from its frozen ``example_snapshot`` (DEV-667).

Per RFC 0003 §7.1 this endpoint never joins to live ``dataset_rows``
or its child tables — the response comes entirely from the
snapshot blob captured at run dequeue time. That guarantees
historical results stay renderable even after the source dataset
row is edited or deleted.

get.description:
Return the recommended-evaluators starter catalog.

A fixed, global, read-only catalog of curated LLM-judge presets the
user browses and then adopts one at a time through the ordinary
``POST /v1/evaluators`` create path — there is no recommended-specific
persistence or mutation behind this surface. Authentication is
inherited from the router's include-level dependency; the catalog is
not project-scoped, so the route takes no ``project_id`` and reads no
database.

Declared ABOVE ``GET /{evaluator_id}`` deliberately: FastAPI matches
routes in declaration order, so a ``/recommended`` registered after
the parameterised route would be shadowed by it (``evaluator_id``
would capture the literal string ``"recommended"``). Keeping this
handler immediately after ``GET ""`` and before the ``/{evaluator_id}``
route is what makes the literal path win.

Get an evaluator by ID, hydrated with run projections.

The detail surface is the canonical place to read
``runs_count`` / ``last_pass_rate`` / ``trend`` — the route
pre-fetches the per-run pass-rate trend from the evaluations
domain (cross-domain reads from the route are the documented
pattern, mirroring ``GET /evaluators/{id}/usage``) and hands the
raw tuples to the service so the service layer stays free of
cross-domain ORM coupling.

patch.description:
Inline-patch an evaluator's user-authored copy + config fields.

Accepts a partial ``{name?, description?, prompt?, subkind?,
scoring_type?, pass_threshold?}`` payload — the narrow surface the
Evaluators table's inline editor, the Evaluator side-panel Criteria
and Overview tabs, and the Evaluator New/Edit form commit.
``prompt`` is gated to ``llm_judge`` evaluators and ``subkind``
to ``structured_match`` evaluators (a mismatched ``kind`` yields a
422); ``scoring_type`` accepts ``pass_fail`` on every kind but
rejects ``scale_1_5`` on a structured_match evaluator with a 422;
``pass_threshold`` is coerced to ``None`` for any evaluator whose
effective scoring is not ``scale_1_5``. ``prompt`` and
``scoring_type`` are version-bump fields — patching either cuts a new
``EvaluatorVersion`` row via the same bump path the full-form editor
uses. The ``enable_reasoning`` toggle round-trips through the
full-form editor at a different surface.

delete.description:
Delete an evaluator. Versions and calibration runs are removed too.

Returns ``409 Conflict`` when an evaluation run that pins this
evaluator is still in flight (``pending`` / ``running``) — deleting
it would orphan the live job. The active-run count is read from the
evaluations domain here (cross-domain imports from ``evaluators/``
are banned, so the route does the read) and handed to the service,
which raises ``EvaluatorInUseError`` inside the same request
transaction the delete would commit in — so the guard is
transaction-isolated, not a best-effort pre-check.

get.description:
Return how many evaluation runs currently reference this evaluator.

The usage count is sourced from the evaluations domain via the route
(cross-domain imports from evaluators/ are banned) so that the delete
confirmation dialog can warn the user before removing an in-use
evaluator.

Return the active / completed run breakdown for the Delete confirm flow.

Drives the Evaluator side-panel's three-state Delete confirm:
``active_run_count > 0`` blocks the delete, a non-zero
``completed_run_count`` warns (past results are preserved), and a
zero ``total_run_count`` is a simple confirm.

Unlike ``GET /{id}/usage`` (a flat ``usage_count``), this surface
splits the runs by status. The breakdown is sourced from the
evaluations domain via the route — cross-domain imports from
``evaluators/`` are banned — and computed in a single aggregate
query, so the FE no longer over-fetches a page of runs to tally the
counts client-side.

post.description:
Calibrate an evaluator against a dataset column's human annotations.

DEV-662 / M4-08 — every annotated row on the
``(dataset_id, column_key)`` pair becomes one calibration sample.
The route pre-fetches the dataset, target column, and joined rows
+ non-null annotations from ``datasets.repository``; the service
layer (``EvaluatorService.calibrate_against_dataset_column``) does
the LLM judging, F1 math, persistence, and version bump.

The judge model is NOT caller-selectable: calibration always runs
``settings.LLM_JUDGE_MODEL`` — the same model the eval-run worker's
judge executes — because the agreement numbers are only predictive
of production verdicts if the two paths are identical. (The legacy
``model`` query parameter, which silently defaulted every
calibration to a model the worker never runs, has been removed
outright rather than kept-and-ignored: no client ever sent it, and
an ignored knob in the OpenAPI surface would imply a choice that
does not exist.)

Raises:
    CalibrationDatasetNotFoundError (404) — the dataset id is unknown.
    CalibrationColumnNotFoundError (404) — the column key is unknown
        on this dataset.
    CalibrationColumnTypeNotSupportedError (422) — column is one of
        score / axial / multi_tag / comment (deferred past M4-08).
    CalibrationNoAnnotationsError (422) — no non-null annotations
        exist on this column yet.
    EvaluatorNotFoundError / StructuredMatchNotSupportedError —
        propagated from the service layer.

get.description:

the Calibrate tab renders fresh and historical runs identically.

Tenant scoping mirrors the sibling evaluator routes: a cross-tenant
or unknown ``evaluator_id`` collapses to the same 404.

AgentVersionResponse.title: AgentVersionResponse
AgentVersionResponse.description:
Wire shape for an agent version row.

DEV-1026 V1 widens the response with rollup fields the new top-level
Agent versions page renders inline:

- ``session_count`` — total sessions pinned to this version (any
  origin: synced / uploaded / simulator).
- ``eval_run_count`` — total evaluation runs pinned to this version.
- ``last_activity_at`` — most recent ``created_at`` across the union
  of pinned sessions + eval runs, or ``None`` when this version has
  neither yet.

Defaults keep the schema backward-compatible: callers that don't need
the rollup (e.g. the simulator picker) just ignore the extra fields,
and DB paths that don't compute them (legacy fixtures, fast-path
creates) get sensible zero values.

AgentVersionRunOption.title: AgentVersionRunOption
AgentVersionRunOption.description:
One agent-version option for the Evaluation New Run picker.

Picker-shaped projection of an :class:`AgentVersion` row — distinct
from :class:`AgentVersionResponse`, which carries the agent-versions
*domain* list shape (rollups, audit fields) used by the detail
surface. The frontend's ``AgentVersionOption`` interface consumes
this 1:1.

Fields:
    id: The agent-version row id — persisted to the run-create
        request's ``agent_version_id``. A real UUID (so a started
        run resolves against the backend, unlike the R2 const's
        non-resolvable fixture ids).
    label: The human-facing version string (``AgentVersion.label``).
    display_name: The conversational display name for prose copy
        ("Chat with {display_name}", "{display_name} is
        responding…"). Prefers the connection's display name — the
        name the operator sees and edits in Project Settings →
        Connections — over the build label, with identifier syntax
        (underscores, the trailing endpoint ``- <qualifier> (vN)``
        suffix) smoothed out for reading. ``label`` stays the
        build's full identity for pickers; ``display_name`` is what
        a sentence calls the agent. See
        :func:`agent_versions.service._run_option_display_name`.
    tag: Optional status badge — see :data:`AgentVersionRunTag`.
    meta: Secondary line. ``"current (prod)"`` for the current
        build; otherwise a relative-time string derived from
        ``created_at`` (e.g. ``"2 weeks ago"``), or the staging
        build's branch label when one is recorded.
    source: The connector / provider system this build is connected
        through — see :class:`AgentVersionRunSource`. Drives the
        picker's per-system grouping (header = ``source.label``).

AgentVersionRunOptionListResponse.title: AgentVersionRunOptionListResponse
AgentVersionRunOptionListResponse.description:
Response for ``GET /agent-versions/run-options``.

``items`` is newest-first so the picker's first option is the
