AgentVersionRunSource.title: AgentVersionRunSource
AgentVersionRunSource.description:
The connector / provider system one agent build is connected through.

The New Run + Playground pickers group builds by *source system* — the
connector each agent is wired through (Bedrock AgentCore live, the dev
mock provider locally, Salesforce Agentforce, …). The picker renders
``label`` as the per-group header; ``key`` is the stable connector
identifier the consumer groups on (never rendered as visible text).

Both fields are always present: ``key`` is the canonical connector slug
(``aws_bedrock_agentcore`` | ``salesforce_agentforce`` | ``mock`` | …),
``label`` is its resolved human display name ("Bedrock AgentCore",
"Salesforce Agentforce", "Mock provider"). Derived from the agent
version's ``source_metadata.source`` — see
:func:`agent_versions.service._run_option_source`.

ApprovalDecision.title: ApprovalDecision
ApprovalDecision.description:
Lifecycle states for a write-tool approval gate.

``PENDING`` is the initial state when the harness emits
``approval.required``. ``APPROVED`` / ``REJECTED`` / ``DECIDE_LATER``
are user-driven via the approval decision endpoint.
``TIMEOUT`` is set by the harness when the approval future doesn't
resolve within ``PADDINGTON_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS``.

``DECIDE_LATER`` is the wire encoding for the prototype's floating ×
dismiss intent — the user neither approves nor rejects; they want
time to think about it. From the model's perspective the write does
NOT execute (same as REJECTED), but the resolution row carries a
distinct discriminator so the FE can render "You dismissed the
request" copy without overloading REJECTED, and so audit history
can tell "user said no" from "user wants to revisit".

The hyphenated wire value (``decide-later``, not ``decide_later``)
matches the FE-side ``ApprovalDecision`` union; keeping the form here
means the StrEnum value round-trips through the Pydantic schema, the
SQL CHECK constraint, and the generated OpenAPI/TS mirror without
any per-layer translation.

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable id of the audit row.

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: Whether this row is a decision or a management mutation.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the event was recorded (newest first in the feed).

request_id.title: Request Id
request_id.description: The request that produced the event, when known.

subject_kind.title: Subject Kind
subject_kind.description: Kind of the subject the decision concerned (e.g. user).

subject_id.title: Subject Id
subject_id.description: Identifier of the subject (e.g. the lowercased email).

resource_type.title: Resource Type
resource_type.description: Type of the resource (e.g. project, workspace).

resource_id.title: Resource Id
resource_id.description: Identifier of the resource the decision concerned.

allowed.title: Allowed
allowed.description: The verdict: true if the action was permitted.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the decision settled (e.g. owner-short-circuit, engine).

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: The management operation (e.g. grant, revoke, create_team).

grantor_kind.title: Grantor Kind
grantor_kind.description: Kind of the principal that performed the mutation.

grantor_id.title: Grantor Id
grantor_id.description: Identifier of the principal that performed the mutation.

grantee.title: Grantee
grantee.description: Who the mutation targeted (a principal or team:<id>).

AuditEventRow.title: AuditEventRow
AuditEventRow.description:
One row of the **Access audit** feed — a settled authorization decision or a management mutation.

Decision columns (``subject_*``, ``action``, ``resource_*``, ``allowed``, ``reason``) are populated
on ``decision`` rows; management columns (``operation``, ``grantor_*``, ``grantee``, ``relation``) on
``management`` rows. ``allowed`` is the verdict (``true`` = permitted); ``reason`` explains a
decision (e.g. an owner short-circuit vs a fine-grained engine result vs a cross-workspace refusal).

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

AuditListResponse.title: AuditListResponse
AuditListResponse.description: A page of the **Access audit** feed, newest first, scoped to the caller's workspace.

BudgetResponse.title: BudgetResponse
BudgetResponse.description: Response schema for a conversation budget.

BulkAddToDatasetRequest.title: BulkAddToDatasetRequest
BulkAddToDatasetRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/examples/bulk``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Materialises Examples (DatasetRows + ExampleTurns)
from the matching Sessions in batches inside one transaction. Bumps
the dataset version once at the end (per the M3 PRD bump matrix).

The ``selection`` envelope re-uses the same Sessions narrowing —
bulk-add-to-dataset reads from Sessions on the source side, so the
discriminator and filter shape both come from the Sessions domain.

new_version.title: New Version
new_version.description: Dataset version after the single bump for this batch.

BulkExportFormat.title: BulkExportFormat
BulkExportFormat.description: Export format for bulk export (DEV-746). CSV only at V0.

BulkExportRequest.title: BulkExportRequest
BulkExportRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/export``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Server-streamed CSV export. The endpoint returns
a ``StreamingResponse`` with chunked transfer encoding so a million
rows never materialise in either FE or BE memory.

op.description: Whether to add or remove the supplied tags.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Tags to add/remove. Must be non-empty.

BulkTagRequest.title: BulkTagRequest
BulkTagRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/bulk-tag``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Single endpoint covers both Mode A and Mode B by
discriminating on ``selection.mode``. Implementation is one
``UPDATE`` per request — never a loop of per-row PATCHes.

Idempotent: adding a tag that's already on a session, or removing
one that isn't, is silently accepted (the SQL ARRAY ops dedupe).

updated_count.title: Updated Count
updated_count.description: Number of session rows touched by the UPDATE.

BulkTagResponse.title: BulkTagResponse
BulkTagResponse.description:
Response from the bulk-tag endpoint.

Returns the count of rows the SQL UPDATE actually touched. Note
that idempotent no-ops (e.g. adding a tag that's already present
on every matching row) still count as touched — Postgres can't
cheaply tell us "this row was updated to the same value." The
count is therefore an upper bound on rows that changed; it's
a lower bound on rows that *matched*.

input_tokens.title: Input Tokens
input_tokens.description: Uncached prompt tokens billed at full rate.

cache_creation_tokens.title: Cache Creation Tokens
cache_creation_tokens.description: Tokens written to the prompt cache on this call (~1.25× base rate).

cache_read_tokens.title: Cache Read Tokens
cache_read_tokens.description: Tokens served from cache on a hit (~0.1× base rate).

cache_hit_rate.title: Cache Hit Rate
cache_hit_rate.description: Fraction 0–1 of cacheable prompt tokens that came from the cache: cache_read / (cache_read + input_tokens). Higher is better; the M7 probe baseline for Paddington turn-2+ is ~0.9.

savings_usd.title: Savings Usd
savings_usd.description: Estimated USD saved vs. a cache-less baseline. Computed as cache_read_tokens × (base_input_price − cache_read_price) for Sonnet at $3/1M in, $0.30/1M read — directional, not billing.

CacheWindow.title: CacheWindow
CacheWindow.description:
Prompt-cache usage over one time window (DEV-559).

Aggregated across every Paddington assistant turn in the window by
summing the cache fields Bedrock returns on the response — the
harness persists them in ``task_turns.token_usage`` already, so
this is a pure DB read with no extra instrumentation.

dataset_id.title: Dataset Id
dataset_id.description: Dataset whose annotations form the calibration ground truth

column_key.title: Column Key
column_key.description: DatasetColumn.key whose annotation values to score against

CalibrateEvaluatorInput.title: CalibrateEvaluatorInput
CalibrateEvaluatorInput.description:
Input for ``POST /evaluators/{id}/calibrate`` (DEV-662 / M4-08).

Calibration runs the evaluator against the human annotations stored
on a specific column of a dataset. Each annotated row becomes one
calibration sample: the human annotation is the ground truth, the
evaluator's verdict on that row's example content is the prediction.

M4-08 ships ``pass_fail`` support only; other column types
(``score`` / ``axial`` / ``multi_tag`` / ``comment``) raise
``CalibrationColumnTypeNotSupportedError`` until follow-ups land.

CalibrationRuleResultResponse.title: CalibrationRuleResultResponse
CalibrationRuleResultResponse.description: Per-rule aggregate metrics in a calibration run.

CalibrationRunResponse.title: CalibrationRunResponse
CalibrationRunResponse.description: Response schema for a calibration run with per-session and per-rule results.

CalibrationSessionResultResponse.title: CalibrationSessionResultResponse
CalibrationSessionResultResponse.description: Per-(session, rule) annotation comparison in a calibration run.

ChangeLedger.title: ChangeLedger
ChangeLedger.description:
The change ledger for one result: the records the agent WROTE, with overreach.

Feeds the result fly-in's change-ledger region — the records the agent
*changed* during the run, each an action + the changed record + an
out-of-scope flag. Derived read-side from a score's agent-authored writes
(``backend_snapshot_actual``) diffed against the scenario's declared scope
(``backend_snapshot_expected``); no column, no migration backs it.

WRITES ONLY. A read leaves no end-state trace and is never projected or
counted here — there is no read entry and no read-count field anywhere in
this shape, by construction.

``has_snapshot`` is the neutral-degrade gate: ``False`` when no backend
snapshot was captured (``capture_state`` / Seam B parked), in which case
``entries`` is empty — the FE renders an empty ledger, never a fabricated
change. It keys off the same ``score_facts_has_snapshot`` signal the
``end_state`` matrix cell and the end-state diff use, so the ledger's "not
captured" degrade stays in lockstep. ``out_of_scope_count`` is the overreach
rollup so a consumer can badge the region without re-walking the entries.

ChangeLedgerEntry.title: ChangeLedgerEntry
ChangeLedgerEntry.description:
One record the agent changed: action + the changed record + overreach flag.

Feeds the FE ``change-ledger-row`` primitive. ``record_name`` is the
connector's record identifier (the snapshot map key — a Case id, a
Salesforce sObject id), which the FE renders as a friendly quoted label.
``action`` is one of the three observable write kinds
(``created`` / ``updated`` / ``deleted``) — there is no ``read`` because a
read is not observable. ``out_of_scope`` is ``True`` when the write fell
outside the scenario's declared record scope (overreach) — a warning flag on
the change, never a verdict (an out-of-scope write does not render a red
fail).

CohortBreakdownRequest.title: CohortBreakdownRequest
CohortBreakdownRequest.description:
Inputs for the Evaluation New Run cohort preview.

The new-run form sends the three picked field values as the user
fills them in; the response splits the chosen dataset's rows into
the cohorts the form's "What this run will evaluate" cards render.

``agent_version_id`` is accepted for forward-compatibility — agent
versions do not gate row inclusion today, but the form already
binds the picker value and a stable request contract means the FE
needn't change when agent-version-aware cohort logic lands.
``evaluator_ids`` is likewise carried for contract stability; the
cohort split is derived entirely from the dataset's pass/fail
annotation column.

agent_testable.title: Agent Testable
agent_testable.description: Rows whose pass/fail verdict is a pass — the run's gradable agent-output cohort.

evaluator_testable.title: Evaluator Testable
evaluator_testable.description: Rows with any pass/fail verdict (pass or fail) — the evaluator-accuracy cohort.

currently_failing.title: Currently Failing
currently_failing.description: Rows whose pass/fail verdict is a fail (a subset of evaluator_testable).

unusable.title: Unusable
unusable.description: Rows with no pass/fail verdict entered — excluded from every testable cohort.

total_rows.title: Total Rows
total_rows.description: The dataset's total row count, for '<N> / <total>' display.

CohortBreakdownResponse.title: CohortBreakdownResponse
CohortBreakdownResponse.description:
Row-cohort counts for one ``(dataset, agent version)`` pairing.

The new-run form renders these as two cards — "Agent pass rate"
(``agent_testable`` / ``total_rows``, with ``currently_failing`` and
``unusable`` as inline notes) and "Evaluator accuracy"
(``evaluator_testable`` / ``total_rows``).

A row's cohort is decided by its value in the dataset's pass/fail
annotation column (the column whose ``type == 'pass_fail'``). When a
dataset has no pass/fail column every row counts as ``unusable``.

ColumnOptionUsageResponse.title: ColumnOptionUsageResponse
ColumnOptionUsageResponse.description:
Wire shape for ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns/{column_key}/option-usage``.

``counts`` maps each option value to the number of dataset rows that
use it — computed dataset-wide via an aggregate query so the count
is truthful regardless of how many rows the client has loaded.

For an ``axial`` annotation column the value is the chosen pole
*key*; for a ``multi_tag`` annotation column each row contributes
one increment per tag in its value list. The sentinel ``column_key``
:data:`SYSTEM_TAGS_USAGE_KEY` aggregates the system row-level
``DatasetRow.tags`` field instead of an annotation column.

Option values with zero usage are intentionally absent from the map
— callers default a missing key to ``0``. ``total_rows`` is the
dataset's row count, surfaced so the modal can phrase the
"N of M rows" copy without a second request.

CreateAgentVersionInput.title: CreateAgentVersionInput
CreateAgentVersionInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /agent-versions``.

``project_id`` scopes the label; ``(project_id, label)`` is unique.

CreateDatasetColumnInput.title: CreateDatasetColumnInput
CreateDatasetColumnInput.description:
Body for ``POST /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns``.

The route layer hands the raw ``config`` dict to the service, which
validates it against the per-type config model (above). We keep
``config`` typed as ``dict[str, Any]`` here rather than a discriminated
union because pydantic-style discriminator wiring at this layer
couples the ``type`` field to a fragile ``Annotated[Union[..]]``;
delegating to the service keeps the validation rules in one place.

``key`` is the stable machine identifier — must match
``[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*`` so it's safe to use in URLs and JSON keys.

CreateDatasetRowAgentStepInput.title: CreateDatasetRowAgentStepInput
CreateDatasetRowAgentStepInput.description:
Agent execution metadata copied onto a row-created ExampleTurn.

This is the single-row counterpart to the sessions bulk materializer:
clients that already have a session detail payload can send
human-readable turns plus their trace/tool metadata in one request
instead of falling back to legacy ``input_data`` pointer blobs.

CreateDatasetRowInput.title: CreateDatasetRowInput
CreateDatasetRowInput.description:
Body for ``POST /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows``.

Per M3-11 (DEV-660), the legacy ``input_data`` / ``expected_output``
JSONB columns were dropped from storage. They're still accepted on
the wire as a backwards-compat affordance: when supplied, the
service translates them into one user turn + one assistant turn
+ ``io_split = 1`` and writes them through the new
``ExampleTurn`` schema. New surfaces can supply ``turns`` +
``io_split`` directly so the row is evaluation-ready immediately.

CreateDatasetRowTurnInput.title: CreateDatasetRowTurnInput
CreateDatasetRowTurnInput.description: A materialized turn copied into a newly-created dataset row.

simulate_at_eval_time.title: Simulate At Eval Time
simulate_at_eval_time.description: Drive a fresh persona+goal simulation per scenario row at evaluation time instead of replaying stored input turns. Requires the row to carry a persona and goal; rows without fall back to replay.

trials.title: Trials
trials.description: Number of independent trials (replications) to run per dataset row, 1-4. Default 1. Higher values repeat each test case to measure reliability, at proportionally higher run time and cost.

subkind.description: Which dimension a non-LLM-judge evaluator compares. For ``structured_match``: ``routedTo`` | ``toolsUsed``. For ``outcome_grounded``: ``safety`` | ``outcome`` | ``quality``. Required when ``kind`` is one of those; must be NULL for ``llm_judge``.

pass_threshold.title: Pass Threshold
pass_threshold.description: 1-5-scale pass cutoff (2-5): a score at or above this value counts as a pass. Meaningful only for llm_judge evaluators scored ``scale_1_5``; coerced to NULL by the service for pass/fail judges and structured_match evaluators.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Evaluation prompt text (required for llm_judge, forbidden otherwise)

rubric.description: Optional structured tailoring of an llm_judge evaluator's criteria — pass/fail rules (``pass_fail``) or 1-5 anchors (``scale_1_5``). When supplied, the service regenerates ``prompt`` from it deterministically, so the prose the judge reads is the assembly of the rubric. Forbidden on non-llm_judge evaluators; the rubric shape must match ``scoring_type``.

enable_reasoning.title: Enable Reasoning
enable_reasoning.description: Enable chain-of-thought reasoning for LLM Judge calls. Off by default (fast, extractive). Turn on for nuanced judgments — adds significant latency and token cost.

source.description: Where the evaluator came from — ``user`` (authored in the app, the default for an ordinary create) or ``recommended`` (adopted from the starter catalog). Durable provenance, set once at create time; never bumped (it is not criteria, so it must not cut an evaluator version).

recommended_key.title: Recommended Key
recommended_key.description: For an adopted recommended evaluator, the stable catalog slug of the preset it came from (e.g. ``tone_professional``). Omitted / NULL for an ordinary ``user`` create — decoupled from the editable name so the adopt surfaces dedupe off the slug, not a name match.

CreateEvaluatorInput.title: CreateEvaluatorInput
CreateEvaluatorInput.description:
Input for creating a new evaluator.

LLM Judge evaluators must supply a prompt. Structured Match evaluators
have fixed comparison logic and must not supply one — they pin a
``subkind`` (``routedTo`` | ``toolsUsed``) to choose the comparison
field.

DEV-681 / M7-03: ``evaluator_type`` was retired here in favour of
the ``kind`` / ``subkind`` pair (DEV-643 / M4-01) — the underlying
column has been dropped from ``evaluation.evaluators``.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Prompt text used for this calibration

CreateEvaluatorVersionInput.title: CreateEvaluatorVersionInput
CreateEvaluatorVersionInput.description: Input for creating a new evaluator version (calibration run result).

CreateExampleTurnInput.title: CreateExampleTurnInput
CreateExampleTurnInput.description:
Insert a new turn into a DatasetRow's example.

``idx`` is optional. When omitted, the new turn is appended at the
end. When provided, existing turns at ``idx`` and beyond are shifted
right by one to make space — this is the only way to *insert* in the
middle of the sequence.

label.title: Label
label.description: Qualitative label for this open code

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: ID of the session being coded

annotation_id.title: Annotation Id
annotation_id.description: ID of the annotation this code relates to

CreatePersonaInput.title: CreatePersonaInput
CreatePersonaInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/personas``.

``project_id`` is sourced from the path parameter; it is intentionally
NOT included here so the body can't accidentally re-parent the persona
to a different project.

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional client-supplied project id (UUID). Defaults to a server-generated UUID when omitted.

agent_platform.title: Agent Platform
agent_platform.description: Primary agent platform this project will evaluate

CreateSessionAnnotationColumnInput.title: CreateSessionAnnotationColumnInput
CreateSessionAnnotationColumnInput.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/annotation-columns``.

``config`` is typed as ``dict[str, Any]`` and validated against the
per-type config model by the service — the same delegation the dataset
column subsystem uses so the validation rules live in one place.

``key`` is the stable machine identifier — must match
``[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*`` so it's safe in URLs and JSON keys.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Project ID this session belongs to

CreateSessionInput.title: CreateSessionInput
CreateSessionInput.description: Input for creating a new session.

persona_id.title: Persona Id
persona_id.description: Optional persona id; null for ad-hoc runs.

scenario_id.title: Scenario Id
scenario_id.description: Optional scenario this run is generated from. When set, the emitted session links back to it and is stamped with a snapshot of the scenario and persona it ran against. Null for ad-hoc runs.

agent_version_id.title: Agent Version Id
agent_version_id.description: The agent build the simulation runs against.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: Scenario-specific objective the simulated user pursues this run (distinct from the persona's standing goals). Threaded into the turn-draft prompt.

provider_kind.title: Provider Kind
provider_kind.description: Deprecated legacy override. New clients omit this; the backend derives provider routing from the selected agent version.

CreateSimulationInput.title: CreateSimulationInput
CreateSimulationInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/simulations``.

The actual provider invoke + streaming flow lives in M11-03; this
body just records the parameters the simulator should run with so
the simulation row exists in ``running`` state with the right FKs
pinned at creation time.

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional stable team id (pass to make creation idempotent on retry).

CreateTeamInput.title: CreateTeamInput
CreateTeamInput.description:
Body for ``POST /workspace/teams`` — create a team with a display name.

An optional ``id`` makes creation **idempotent on retry**: re-creating an existing id re-sets the
same name (the resolver's contract — no error, no duplicate). Omitted ⇒ the resolver mints one.

DashboardResponse.title: DashboardResponse
DashboardResponse.description: Aggregated dashboard data powering the overview page.

DatasetColumnResponse.title: DatasetColumnResponse
DatasetColumnResponse.description:
Wire shape for a DatasetColumn.

``config`` is returned as the raw dict — clients are expected to
dispatch on ``type`` and read the type-specific keys. The server
has already validated the shape at write time.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

DatasetRowListResponse.title: DatasetRowListResponse
DatasetRowListResponse.description: Paginated list of dataset rows / examples (DEV-750).

turns.title: Turns
turns.description: Structured per-turn rows for the example. Populated only when ?include=turns is set on the row read endpoints; ``null`` otherwise. Mirrors the Sessions detail endpoint's ``?include=turns`` projection — each entry carries idx / role / content plus an ordered ``agent_steps`` array.

source_session.description: Per-row projection of the live source Session for a row materialised from a session — transcript preview, routed-to topics, tools called, turn count, session date, originating user, and duration. ``null`` for uploaded rows and for rows whose source session has since been deleted.

DatasetRowResponse.title: DatasetRowResponse
DatasetRowResponse.description:
Wire shape for a DatasetRow.

Post-M3-11 (DEV-660), the legacy ``input_data`` / ``expected_output``
JSONB columns were dropped from the database. This response shape
keeps the keys for backwards compatibility — they're populated
server-side from the row's turns + ``io_split`` via
:mod:`legacy_io` when the service layer serialises the row.
Frontend callers that already render those keys (the dataset
detail row preview, the evaluation result panel) keep working
without a change. New surfaces should prefer ``turns`` +
``io_split`` directly.

DatasetTagVocabularyResponse.title: DatasetTagVocabularyResponse
DatasetTagVocabularyResponse.description:
Wire shape for ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/tag-vocabulary``.

``tags`` is the dataset-wide row-tag vocabulary, sorted
case-insensitively. It is the union of the dataset's *stored*
vocabulary (``Dataset.tag_vocabulary``) and every tag currently in
use on the dataset's rows — so the vocabulary is never narrower than
the data, and a tag stays selectable even after a row that used it
is filtered out of the loaded set.

``usage_counts`` maps each vocabulary tag to the number of rows that
currently carry it (zero for a stored-but-unused tag).

DatasetVersionEventListResponse.title: DatasetVersionEventListResponse
DatasetVersionEventListResponse.description:
Paginated wire shape for ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/events``.

``items`` is in ``created_at DESC`` order — newest event first —
so the timeline UI can render top-down without re-sorting.
``total`` reflects the count *after* the optional ``kind`` filter
is applied so paging math is correct in either mode.

DatasetVersionEventResponse.title: DatasetVersionEventResponse
DatasetVersionEventResponse.description:
Wire shape for one row in ``dataset_version_events``.

Emitted by ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/events`` and consumed by
the dataset version-history viewer (M6-13). Mirrors the columns
on :class:`DatasetVersionEvent` exactly — no service-layer
reshaping. Clients dispatch on ``kind`` to decide how to render
the ``before_json`` / ``after_json`` slices.

The ``row_id`` field is logically a reference to ``DatasetRow.id``
but intentionally NOT a foreign key — the audit log survives row
deletions so historical bumps stay visible. Clients should treat
a missing row gracefully.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: Plain-language description of what the evaluator should check.

agent_context.title: Agent Context
agent_context.description: Optional short description of the agent being evaluated, used to tailor the rules.

scoring_hint.description: Optional nudge toward a scoring shape (``pass_fail`` | ``scale_1_5``). The model may override it when the goal clearly implies the other shape.

DraftEvaluatorRulesInput.title: DraftEvaluatorRulesInput
DraftEvaluatorRulesInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/evaluators/draft-rules``.

AI-assisted drafting. The user supplies a plain-language ``goal``
("check the agent never promises a refund it can't authorize"); the
service layer calls Bifrost and returns a populated set of scoring
rules the FE renders into the evaluator create form for review. Does
NOT persist — saving goes through the existing CRUD on the user's
confirmation. ``agent_context`` is an optional short description of
the agent under test so the rules can be tailored to it.
``scoring_hint`` is an optional nudge toward a scoring shape; the
model is free to override it when the goal clearly implies the other
shape.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: One-line description of the persona to draft.

DraftPersonaInput.title: DraftPersonaInput
DraftPersonaInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/personas/draft``.

DEV-769 / M11-06: AI-assisted drafting. The user supplies a one-line
goal ("frustrated long-time customer trying to cancel"); the service
layer calls Bifrost and returns a populated persona shape the FE can
render into the create form for review. Does NOT persist — saving
goes through the existing CRUD on the user's confirmation.

scenario_id.title: Scenario Id
scenario_id.description: Optional scenario to link this finalised run to. When set, the emitted session links back to it and is stamped with a snapshot of the scenario and persona it ran against. Null leaves the session unlinked.

EndSimulationInput.title: EndSimulationInput
EndSimulationInput.description:
Optional request body for ``POST /v1/simulations/{id}/end``.

Carries the after-the-fact scenario link the playground sets when the
user saves an already-run conversation as a scenario. Omitting the
body (or sending an empty one) keeps the interactive simulator's
finalisation byte-unchanged — no scenario stamp, no provenance.

EndSimulationResponse.title: EndSimulationResponse
EndSimulationResponse.description:
Returned from ``POST /v1/simulations/{id}/end``.

``session_id`` is the ID the FE redirects to so the operator can
review the just-emitted session.

EndStateDiff.title: EndStateDiff
EndStateDiff.description:
The end-state diff for one result: per-record/per-field expected vs actual.

Feeds the ``state-diff-card`` hero and the result fly-in's end-state region
— the *what differed* companion to the ``end_state`` matrix cell's *whether
it differed*. Derived read-side from a
score's ``backend_snapshot_expected`` vs ``backend_snapshot_actual`` JSONB;
no column, no migration backs it.

``has_snapshot`` is the neutral-degrade gate: ``False`` when no backend
snapshot was captured (``capture_state`` / Seam B parked), in which case
``records`` is empty and ``mismatch`` is ``False`` — the FE renders "End
state not captured", never a red diff. It keys off the same
``score_facts_has_snapshot`` signal the ``end_state`` matrix cell uses, so
the diff's "not captured" degrade stays in lockstep with the cell. When a
snapshot was captured, ``records`` carries the per-record diffs and
``mismatch`` is the overall rollup.

EndStateFieldDiff.title: EndStateFieldDiff
EndStateFieldDiff.description:
One backend field's expected vs actual value, with a mismatch flag.

The leaf of the end-state diff (the ``state-diff-card`` hero / the result
fly-in's end-state region): per field, the value the scenario ``expected``
against the value actually captured (``actual``), and a ``mismatch`` flag
that is ``True`` whenever the two differ — including a field present on only
one side (the absent side renders the "—" marker, and an absent value IS a
difference). Both sides are human strings — a backend field can be a
string, number, bool, or nested blob, and the diff cell is a human
comparison, not a typed value (AGENTS.md "Built for Humans and Agents").

EndStateRecordDiff.title: EndStateRecordDiff
EndStateRecordDiff.description:
One backend record's field-by-field diff, with a per-record mismatch rollup.

``record_name`` is the connector's record identifier (the snapshot map key —
a Case id, a Salesforce sObject id, a DynamoDB item key). ``fields`` is the
union of the record's fields across the expected and actual snapshots, in
stable order. ``mismatch`` is the per-record rollup — ``True`` when any
field mismatched.

EnsureProjectInput.title: EnsureProjectInput
EnsureProjectInput.description:
Body for ``PUT /v1/projects/{project_id}`` — idempotent provision.

The id is the path parameter. These attributes are used only when the
project is created; they're ignored when it already exists (ensure is
get-or-create, never an update).

total_runs.title: Total Runs
total_runs.description: Count of all evaluation runs in the project, any status.

runs_last_7d.title: Runs Last 7D
runs_last_7d.description: Count of runs completed within the last 7 days (completed status only).

avg_pass_rate.title: Avg Pass Rate
avg_pass_rate.description: Row-weighted average pass rate (0.0-1.0) across all result rows of completed runs in the project. Null when no completed run has any scored results.

EvaluationProjectStatsResponse.title: EvaluationProjectStatsResponse
EvaluationProjectStatsResponse.description:
Project-wide aggregate stats for the Evaluations list page header.

The Evaluations list renders a dot-separated subtitle —
"<N> total runs · <N> in last 7 days · <X> avg pass rate · last 7 days"
(see docs/design/evaluations-list/notes.md §"Page header"). These
three fields back that line. They are computed server-side over the
*whole* project — never page-scoped — because the list endpoint is
paginated and a page-scoped denominator would understate the stats
on every page but the first.

A separate endpoint (rather than folding these onto the list
response) keeps the stats page-independent: the list can re-paginate
without forcing a re-aggregation, and the FE fetches the two
concerns on independent cadences.

* ``total_runs`` — count of ALL runs in the project regardless of
  status (matches the design's plain "N runs" subtitle).
* ``runs_last_7d`` — count of runs *completed* within the last 7
  days (status ``completed`` only; the 7d window anchors on
  ``completed_at``).
* ``avg_pass_rate`` — row-weighted average pass rate across every
  result row of every completed run in the project ("% of examples
  that passed"). ``None`` for a project with no scored completed
  runs. See ``aggregate_project_stats`` for the denominator
  rationale.

ord.title: Ord
ord.description: Zero-based step ordinal within the turn (sorted ASC).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Step kind discriminator: one of 'route', 'tool', 'llm', 'reasoning', or 'model_call'.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label — route name, tool name, or model name.

EvaluationResultAgentStepProjection.title: EvaluationResultAgentStepProjection
EvaluationResultAgentStepProjection.description:
One agent execution step within a projected transcript turn.

Mirrors :class:`src.domain.sessions.schemas.SessionAgentStepResponse`
so the FE's shared ``<Transcript>`` primitive renders the evaluation
Result fly-in's Transcript tab with the exact same component tree it
uses for live sessions. Sourced from ``example_snapshot["agent_steps"]``
(RFC 0003 §4.2) — never from the live ``example_agent_steps`` table.

EvaluationResultDetailResponse.title: EvaluationResultDetailResponse
EvaluationResultDetailResponse.description:
Per-result detail rendered from the frozen ``example_snapshot``.

Per RFC 0003 §7.1 ("Detail endpoint"), this response **must not**
join to live ``dataset_rows`` / ``example_turns`` /
``example_agent_steps`` / ``dataset_row_annotations``. Everything
the detail page renders comes from the snapshot blob captured at
run dequeue time, which means a result row stays renderable even
after its source dataset row is edited or deleted.

``example_snapshot`` is typed as a free-form dict because its
inner shape lives in RFC 0003 — the FE consumes it through a
typed wrapper generated from the OpenAPI schema, but the FastAPI
surface keeps it opaque so future RFC-compatible field additions
don't trigger an OpenAPI / type regeneration churn.

``turns`` is the structured transcript projection: the snapshot's
``turns[]`` array with each turn's ``agent_steps`` joined back under
it by ``turn_id``. It exists so the evaluation Result fly-in's
Transcript tab can render the real ``<Transcript>`` primitive
instead of degrading to raw Input/Expected/Actual payload blocks.
It is a read-side reshape of ``example_snapshot`` — no live join,
so the RFC 0003 §7.1 contract holds. Empty list (never null) for
legacy results whose snapshot pre-dates the turn capture, or that
have no snapshot at all.

idx.title: Idx
idx.description: Zero-based turn index within the example.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Conversational per-turn timestamp, frozen from the source ``ExampleTurn.recorded_at`` at run-dequeue snapshot time. ``null`` for turns with no conversational source (CSV-uploaded / turn-spec / manually-inserted examples) and for legacy snapshots frozen before the field existed — the FE renders the role word alone in that case. Mirrors ``SessionTurnResponse.timestamp``.

intents.title: Intents
intents.description: Router intents/skills recorded for this turn, when the frozen snapshot carried them (``intents`` or ``routedTo`` key). ``null`` when the snapshot has no routing signal for the turn.

agent_steps.title: Agent Steps
agent_steps.description: Ordered agent execution steps that produced this turn, sorted by ``ord`` ASC. Empty list (never null) when the snapshot recorded no steps for the turn.

EvaluationResultTurnProjection.title: EvaluationResultTurnProjection
EvaluationResultTurnProjection.description:
One conversation turn projected from a result's frozen snapshot.

The evaluation Result fly-in's Transcript tab needs a real turn
list, not just Input/Expected/Actual payload blocks. RFC 0003
§4.1/§4.2 already require ``example_snapshot`` to carry a full deep
copy of the source example's ``turns[]`` and ``agent_steps[]``, so
this projection is a pure **read-side reshape** of the frozen blob
— it never joins live ``example_turns`` / ``example_agent_steps``
and so stays fully within the RFC 0003 §7.1 "detail endpoint never
joins live" contract.

Shape mirrors :class:`src.domain.sessions.schemas.SessionTurnResponse`
so the FE can feed it straight into the shared ``<Transcript>``
primitive without a shape adapter.

EvaluationRunAgentVersionAggregate.title: EvaluationRunAgentVersionAggregate
EvaluationRunAgentVersionAggregate.description:
One row of the aggregate-by-agent-version response.

Each row represents all evaluation runs in the project pinned to the
same ``agent_version_id``. ``avg_pass_rate`` is the simple mean of
each run's pass rate (``passed_rows / completed_rows``); runs with
``completed_rows == 0`` are excluded from the mean (and surface as
``None`` if no run in the bucket has progressed). The label is
pulled from the ``agent_versions`` table for human display; legacy
runs with ``agent_version_id IS NULL`` report ``None`` for both id
and label.

source.title: Source
source.description:
What kind of input this run was driven from.

A read-time discriminator the FE uses to badge a run and pick the
right launcher/round-trip vocabulary — derived, never stored, so
no column and no migration back it. The seam from the Scenarios
redesign is "read a run-level marker if one was landed, else
derive from ``simulate_at_eval_time``": no such marker column
exists on ``evaluation_runs``, so this derives.

``"scenario"`` when the run drove a fresh persona+goal simulation
per row at evaluation time (``simulate_at_eval_time``); otherwise
``"session"`` — the run replayed each row's stored conversation
turns. This is a machine discriminator: the FE renders its own
humanised label from it and never surfaces the raw token.

status_label.title: Status Label
status_label.description:
Human display copy for ``status`` — the badge's rendered text.

The raw ``status`` enum value stays on the wire for the machine;
this is the human copy a run-status badge renders. The lifecycle
vocab humanises ``PENDING`` → "Queued" (an enqueued run that has
not started is "Queued", not "Pending"), keeps Running, and
spells the terminal states "Completed" / "Failed" / "Cancelled".
Derived, never stored — no column, no migration backs it. Total
over the closed enum set so a future status can't render blank.

cancel_requested.title: Cancel Requested
cancel_requested.description:
Whether an operator has requested cancellation of this run.

Derived from the existing ``cancel_requested_at`` marker (stamped
when an operator hits ``POST /evaluations/{run_id}/cancel``): True
once the marker is set, so the FE can show a "Cancelling…" state
distinct from the terminal ``CANCELED`` status while a still-
``RUNNING`` run drains its in-flight rows. Once the run reaches
the terminal cancelled state ``status`` reads ``canceled`` and
this stays True. A read-time projection over a column that
already exists — no new column, no migration.

EvaluationRunDetailResponse.title: EvaluationRunDetailResponse
EvaluationRunDetailResponse.description:
Full run + per-result rows for the run-detail page.

Streaming / poll contract: the run-detail page polls this endpoint
while ``status == running`` to drive its live "evaluating" sub-phase.
Each poll re-reads the run row off the database (no caching that
would staleness the running view), so the trial-progress counters —
``completed_rows`` / ``passed_rows`` against the ``total_trials``
denominator — and the ``cancel_requested`` marker are fresh on every
fetch. The FE stops polling once ``status`` is terminal
(``completed`` / ``failed`` / ``canceled``). The humanised
``status_label`` and the ``cancel_requested`` boolean let the page
render "Queued" / "Running" / "Cancelling…" sub-states without
re-deriving the lifecycle vocabulary client-side.

EvaluationRunEvaluatorPassRate.title: EvaluationRunEvaluatorPassRate
EvaluationRunEvaluatorPassRate.description:
One evaluator's pass rate on a single run.

``pass_rate`` is the mean of ``EvaluationScore.verdict == 'pass'``
across every result row in the run that this evaluator scored,
expressed as a fraction in ``[0.0, 1.0]`` — the same 0..1 scale as
the run-level :attr:`EvaluationRunResponse.overall_pass_rate`. It is
``None`` when the run has no ``EvaluationScore`` rows for this
evaluator yet (in-flight runs, or runs that errored before scoring).

Surfaced inside :class:`EvaluationRunSummary` so the Evaluator
side-panel Runs tab can look up *this* evaluator's score per run —
a different question than the run's aggregate ``overall_pass_rate``,
which spans every pinned evaluator.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

source.title: Source
source.description:
What kind of input this run was driven from.

A read-time discriminator the FE uses to badge a run and pick the
right launcher/round-trip vocabulary — derived, never stored, so
no column and no migration back it. The seam from the Scenarios
redesign is "read a run-level marker if one was landed, else
derive from ``simulate_at_eval_time``": no such marker column
exists on ``evaluation_runs``, so this derives.

``"scenario"`` when the run drove a fresh persona+goal simulation
per row at evaluation time (``simulate_at_eval_time``); otherwise
``"session"`` — the run replayed each row's stored conversation
turns. This is a machine discriminator: the FE renders its own
humanised label from it and never surfaces the raw token.

status_label.title: Status Label
status_label.description:
Human display copy for ``status`` — the badge's rendered text.

The raw ``status`` enum value stays on the wire for the machine;
this is the human copy a run-status badge renders. The lifecycle
vocab humanises ``PENDING`` → "Queued" (an enqueued run that has
not started is "Queued", not "Pending"), keeps Running, and
spells the terminal states "Completed" / "Failed" / "Cancelled".
Derived, never stored — no column, no migration backs it. Total
over the closed enum set so a future status can't render blank.

cancel_requested.title: Cancel Requested
cancel_requested.description:
Whether an operator has requested cancellation of this run.

Derived from the existing ``cancel_requested_at`` marker (stamped
when an operator hits ``POST /evaluations/{run_id}/cancel``): True
once the marker is set, so the FE can show a "Cancelling…" state
distinct from the terminal ``CANCELED`` status while a still-
``RUNNING`` run drains its in-flight rows. Once the run reaches
the terminal cancelled state ``status`` reads ``canceled`` and
this stays True. A read-time projection over a column that
already exists — no new column, no migration.

EvaluationRunSummary.title: EvaluationRunSummary
EvaluationRunSummary.description:
Per-run aggregate breakdowns that are not flat scalar fields.

Lets the run-list response carry a per-evaluator pass-rate split
alongside the existing flat ``overall_pass_rate``. ``evaluators``
has one entry per evaluator that produced at least one score on the
run; the Evaluator panel keys into it by ``evaluator_id`` to render
the active evaluator's score in the Runs tab "Pass rate" column.

Empty ``evaluators`` list (never ``None``) when the run has no
scored results yet — the FE renders that as a degraded cell.

EvaluationStatus.title: EvaluationStatus
EvaluationStatus.description:
Lifecycle state of an evaluation run.

``CANCELED`` is a terminal state set when an operator cancels an
in-flight run via ``POST /evaluations/{run_id}/cancel`` (Wave 5).
It is reachable only from ``PENDING`` or ``RUNNING`` — a run that
has already reached ``COMPLETED`` / ``FAILED`` / ``CANCELED`` is
not cancelable.

EvaluatorKind.title: EvaluatorKind
EvaluatorKind.description:
Top-level evaluator taxonomy.

LLM Judge evaluators score with a prompt; structured match evaluators
compare a specific field on the actual turn against the expected turn;
outcome-grounded evaluators score against the connector backend state
captured at run time (the actual vs expected end-state snapshot) — the
foundation of the state-grounded results work.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

EvaluatorListResponse.title: EvaluatorListResponse
EvaluatorListResponse.description:
Paginated list of evaluators (DEV-750).

M8-37 added pagination to ``GET /v1/evaluators`` so the FE
Evaluators table consumes the same generic primitives as Sessions /
Datasets / Evaluations. Wraps the ``items`` array in the shared
``{items, page, page_size, total, total_pages}`` envelope.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Evaluation prompt text — the judge criteria (LLM Judge only). Gated at the service layer to ``llm_judge`` evaluators; a prompt patch on a structured_match evaluator yields a 422. As a ``_VERSION_BUMP_FIELDS`` member, an inline prompt edit cuts a new ``EvaluatorVersion`` row, the same as the full-form editor.

rubric.description: Structured tailoring of the judge criteria (LLM Judge only). Gated at the service layer to ``llm_judge`` evaluators; the rubric shape must match the evaluator's effective ``scoring_type``. When a rubric patch lands the service regenerates ``prompt`` from it via ``assemble_prompt`` — so a rubric edit moves ``prompt`` and cuts a new ``EvaluatorVersion`` row through the existing prompt-based bump, exactly as a hand-edited prompt would.

subkind.description: Which structured field a structured_match evaluator compares (``routedTo`` | ``toolsUsed``). Gated at the service layer to ``structured_match`` evaluators.

scoring_type.description: How the evaluator reports scores (``pass_fail`` | ``scale_1_5``). A ``_VERSION_BUMP_FIELDS`` member — changing it cuts a new ``EvaluatorVersion`` row via the same bump path the full-form editor uses. ``scale_1_5`` is gated at the service layer to ``llm_judge`` evaluators; a ``scale_1_5`` patch on a structured_match evaluator yields a 422.

pass_threshold.title: Pass Threshold
pass_threshold.description: 1-5-scale pass cutoff (2-5). The service coerces it to NULL when the evaluator's effective ``scoring_type`` is not ``scale_1_5`` (pass/fail judges and every structured_match evaluator). When a ``scoring_type`` patch lands in the same request, the coercion runs against that post-patch value.

enable_reasoning.title: Enable Reasoning
enable_reasoning.description: Step-by-step judge reasoning toggle (LLM Judge only). The Evaluator New/Edit form's reasoning switch persists through here. Gated at the service layer to ``llm_judge`` evaluators — structured_match comparisons are deterministic and have no judge to reason — so a reasoning patch on a structured_match evaluator yields a 422. NOT a ``_VERSION_BUMP_FIELDS`` member: flipping it changes how the verdict is produced, not the criteria content, mirroring the ``UpdateEvaluatorInput`` path's no-bump behaviour for the same field.

EvaluatorPatchRequest.title: EvaluatorPatchRequest
EvaluatorPatchRequest.description:
Narrow inline-edit payload for ``PATCH /v1/evaluators/{id}``.

The Evaluators table's inline editor commits the user-authored copy
fields (``name``, ``description``); the Evaluator side-panel Criteria
tab commits the judge ``prompt`` here; the Evaluator panel's
Structured Match config additionally commits ``subkind`` (Routing
match / Tool match). The ``pass_threshold`` cutoff (the 1-5-scale
LLM-judge pass bar) is also patchable so the Evaluator New/Edit
form's threshold control can persist. The Evaluator side-panel
Overview tab's Score type radio additionally commits ``scoring_type``
(pass/fail ↔ scale_1_5) here — see the version-bump note below. The
immutable taxonomy (``kind``) round-trips through the dedicated
full-form editor instead.

Length caps mirror :data:`EVALUATOR_NAME_MAX_LENGTH` /
:data:`EVALUATOR_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH`. ``prompt`` is gated at
the service layer to ``llm_judge`` evaluators — structured_match
rows have fixed comparison logic and carry no prompt — and,
symmetrically, ``subkind`` is gated to the kinds that carry one
(``structured_match`` / ``outcome_grounded``); an ``llm_judge`` row
must keep a NULL subkind, per the ``ck_evaluators_subkind_matches_kind``
CHECK.

``prompt`` and ``scoring_type`` are ``_VERSION_BUMP_FIELDS`` members —
unlike the pure copy fields, mutating either cuts a new
``EvaluatorVersion`` row. ``patch_evaluator`` routes them through the
*same* version-bump path ``update_evaluator`` uses, so an inline
judge-prompt edit or Score-type edit shows up in version history
exactly as a full-form edit would. The ``scale_1_5`` value is gated
at the service layer to ``llm_judge`` evaluators (structured_match
rows are always pass/fail); a ``scale_1_5`` patch on a
structured_match evaluator is rejected with a 422.

EvaluatorPin.title: EvaluatorPin
EvaluatorPin.description:
One ``{id, version}`` entry from ``evaluation_runs.evaluators_pinned``.

Surfaced on ``EvaluationRunResponse`` (DEV-693 / M6-09) so the run
detail page can render a per-evaluator version chip without
making a second round trip to the evaluator-versions endpoint.
The shape mirrors the JSONB row written by the service at run start
(DEV-661 / M4-04).

active_run_count.title: Active Run Count
active_run_count.description: Runs in a ``pending`` / ``running`` state that pin this evaluator. A non-zero count blocks the delete.

completed_run_count.title: Completed Run Count
completed_run_count.description: Runs in a ``completed`` state that pin this evaluator.

total_run_count.title: Total Run Count
total_run_count.description: Every run that pins this evaluator — active + completed + failed. A ``failed`` run is neither active nor completed but still counts toward the total.

EvaluatorRefCheckResponse.title: EvaluatorRefCheckResponse
EvaluatorRefCheckResponse.description:
Referential-check breakdown for the Evaluator Delete confirm flow.

``GET /v1/evaluators/{id}/ref-check`` splits the runs that pin an
evaluator into active vs completed so the side-panel Delete confirm
can pick one of its three states:

* ``active_run_count > 0`` — a run is still in flight; the delete is
  BLOCKED until the user cancels or waits for it.
* ``completed_run_count > 0`` (with no active runs) — the delete is
  ALLOWED but warned: past results stay intact.
* ``total_run_count == 0`` — nothing references the evaluator; a
  simple confirm.

Unlike ``EvaluatorUsageResponse`` (a single flat ``usage_count``),
this surface carries the status breakdown the three-state dialog
needs. The counts are computed server-side with one aggregate query
so the FE no longer has to over-fetch a page of runs and tally the
``evaluators_pinned`` manifests client-side.

rubric.description: Structured tailoring of the judge criteria, when authored. ``None`` when the evaluator carries only hand-authored prose ``prompt`` (the back-compat default). When present, ``prompt`` is the deterministic assembly of this rubric — the prose the judge reads is always reproducible from the structured rows.

pass_threshold.title: Pass Threshold
pass_threshold.description: 1-5-scale pass cutoff (2-5): a score at or above this value counts as a pass. Non-NULL only for llm_judge evaluators scored ``scale_1_5``; ``None`` for pass/fail judges and structured_match evaluators.

source.description: Where the evaluator came from — ``user`` or ``recommended``. A NULL on the persisted row (a pre-column evaluator) reads back as ``user`` here, so the wire surface never carries the NULL — the absence of provenance is indistinguishable from a user-authored evaluator, which is the correct default.

recommended_key.title: Recommended Key
recommended_key.description: The stable catalog slug of the preset an adopted recommended evaluator came from; ``None`` for ``user`` evaluators. The durable marker the adopt surfaces dedupe + decouple attach-from-adopt against — never rendered raw.

criteria_updated_at.title: Criteria Updated At
criteria_updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last change to the evaluator's *criteria* — the judge prompt / rubric / scoring type — as distinct from ``updated_at``, which bumps on EVERY edit including pure copy (name / one-line description). Computed as the most recent ``EvaluatorVersion.created_at`` cut by a genuine criteria edit, falling back to ``created_at`` when the criteria has not been edited since authoring. Forced calibration version bumps — which re-snapshot the prompt with an F1 score WITHOUT changing the rubric — are excluded, so a fresh calibration never makes itself look stale. The Calibrate tab compares a run's check time against this field (NOT ``updated_at``), so renaming or rewording a calibrated evaluator no longer trips a false "Criteria changed since this check" banner. Populated by the service projection on every response; the ``None`` default only guards the ``model_validate`` source row, which carries no such column.

created_by_user.description: Display projection of the WorkOS user who created the evaluator — powers the Evaluator detail Overview tab's "Created by" row. Resolved from the raw ``created_by`` user id via ``_shared/users.resolve`` on the GET detail path only; ``None`` on the create / patch / list responses, for evaluators that predate the ``created_by`` column (re-added in migration 0095), and whenever the author can't be resolved (user removed from WorkOS, or a transient WorkOS failure) — the FE renders a muted dash, never the banned "Workspace member" placeholder. The raw ``created_by`` user id is intentionally NOT surfaced on this response — evaluators have no raw-id consumer, and AGENTS.md "Built for Humans and Agents" bans raw ids in user-facing payloads.

runs_count.title: Runs Count
runs_count.description: Number of EvaluationRun rows that pin this evaluator. On the LIST surface this counts only ``completed`` runs; on the GET detail surface it counts every run in the trend window.

last_pass_rate.title: Last Pass Rate
last_pass_rate.description: Pass-rate of this evaluator on its most recent run; ``None`` when no run has produced a score yet (the design renders this as "Never run"). On the LIST surface this is the row-weighted mean of ``passed_all`` over the most recently completed run; on the GET detail surface it is the newest non-empty point of the per-run pass-rate trend.

trend.title: Trend
trend.description: Per-run pass-rate history, newest first. Capped at the service-level window so the response stays bounded.

EvaluatorResponse.title: EvaluatorResponse
EvaluatorResponse.description:
Response schema for an evaluator.

DEV-681 / M7-03: ``evaluator_type`` was removed from this surface
after the underlying column was dropped — clients should read
``kind`` (and, for structured matches, ``subkind``).

Projections (``runs_count`` / ``last_pass_rate`` / ``trend``) are
computed by the service against the evaluations domain. They are
``0`` / ``None`` / ``[]`` on the create + patch responses where no
runs have referenced the evaluator yet.

``runs_count`` and ``last_pass_rate`` are populated on both the GET
detail surface and the LIST surface — the Evaluators list page
renders a "Last pass rate" column, so ``GET /v1/evaluators`` runs
a batched projection over the evaluations domain. ``trend`` stays a
detail-only field (the side-panel sparkline) and is always ``[]``
on the list response.

lead_in.title: Lead In
lead_in.description: Opening framing paragraph the assembled criteria open with ("Judge whether…" / "Rate how…"). Stored on the rubric so a persisted rubric regenerates its full criteria prose alone.

pass_rules.title: Pass Rules
pass_rules.description: Pass/fail rubric: the clauses completing each ``PASS when …`` criteria line. One line per entry. Mutually exclusive with ``anchors``.

fail_rules.title: Fail Rules
fail_rules.description: Pass/fail rubric: the clauses completing each ``FAIL when …`` criteria line. One line per entry. Mutually exclusive with ``anchors``.

anchors.description: 1-5-scale rubric: the three pinned 5 / 3 / 1 anchors completing each ``Rate N when …`` criteria line. Mutually exclusive with ``pass_rules`` / ``fail_rules``.

guidance.title: Guidance
guidance.description: Optional trailing paragraph closing the assembled criteria prose (e.g. how to interpret intermediate scale points, or what the judge should explicitly ignore). Omitted from the prose when ``None``.

EvaluatorRubric.title: EvaluatorRubric
EvaluatorRubric.description:
The structured tailoring of an LLM-judge evaluator's criteria.

Two disjoint shapes, discriminated by which fields are present (and, on
save, validated against the evaluator's ``scoring_type``):

* **pass/fail** — ``pass_rules`` + ``fail_rules``: the clauses that
  complete the ``PASS when …`` / ``FAIL when …`` lines. Each list entry
  becomes one line, so a rubric with one pass clause and one fail clause
  reproduces the single-PASS-line / single-FAIL-line shape the starter
  catalog's hand-written pass/fail prompts use.
* **scale_1_5** — ``anchors``: the three pinned 5 / 3 / 1 anchors that
  complete the ``Rate N when …`` lines.

The two shapes are mutually exclusive: a pass/fail rubric carries no
``anchors`` and a scale rubric carries no ``pass_rules`` / ``fail_rules``.
Exactly one family must be present — an empty rubric, or one that mixes
both families, is rejected.

``lead_in`` is the opening framing paragraph the assembled criteria open
with ("Judge whether…" / "Rate how…"). It is stored on the rubric so a
persisted rubric is self-sufficient — the save path can regenerate the
full criteria prose from the rubric alone, with no out-of-band lead-in.

``guidance`` is the optional trailing paragraph that closes the criteria
prose (the "judge only the tone, not the correctness" / "use the
intermediate values 2 and 4" closer the hand-written prompts carry). It
applies to both shapes; ``None`` omits the trailing paragraph entirely.

pass_threshold.title: Pass Threshold
pass_threshold.description: 1-5-scale pass cutoff (2-5): a score at or above this value counts as a pass. Non-NULL only when ``scoring_type`` is ``scale_1_5``; ``None`` for ``pass_fail``.

pass_rules.title: Pass Rules
pass_rules.description: Plain-language rules describing what a passing response does.

fail_rules.title: Fail Rules
fail_rules.description: Plain-language rules describing what a failing response does.

prompt_version.title: Prompt Version
prompt_version.description: Versioned prompt id; bump in code on any structural change.

EvaluatorRulesDraftResponse.title: EvaluatorRulesDraftResponse
EvaluatorRulesDraftResponse.description:
Returned from ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/evaluators/draft-rules``.

An UN-PERSISTED draft of an evaluator's plain-language scoring rules.
The FE renders this into the evaluator create form so the user can
review and edit before saving via the existing ``POST /v1/evaluators``
create path. ``pass_rules`` / ``fail_rules`` are short, editable
plain-language bullet lists; ``pass_threshold`` is non-NULL only when
``scoring_type`` is ``scale_1_5``.

EvaluatorSource.title: EvaluatorSource
EvaluatorSource.description:
Where an evaluator came from — its durable provenance.

Recorded once at create time and never bumped (provenance is not
criteria — it must never cut an ``EvaluatorVersion``). The two values:

* ``user`` — authored directly in the app (the New/Edit form, the
  scenario picker's plain "Add evaluator" adder, an API create).
* ``recommended`` — adopted from the recommended-evaluators starter
  catalog. An adopted preset stays a perfectly ordinary, fully
  editable evaluator; ``source`` is the durable marker that it
  ORIGINATED from a preset, and ``recommended_key`` pins WHICH preset
  (the catalog's stable slug, decoupled from the editable name) so the
  two adopt surfaces can dedupe + decouple attach-from-adopt without
  relying on a name match.

The column is nullable on the row: rows that predate it read back as
``user`` (the schema documents the NULL → ``user`` convention), so
there is no backfill.

EvaluatorSubkind.title: EvaluatorSubkind
EvaluatorSubkind.description:
Which dimension a non-LLM-judge evaluator compares.

Two disjoint families share this column, discriminated by ``kind``:

* ``structured_match`` — which structured field on the turn
  to compare. ``ROUTED_TO`` covers the legacy ``intent_match`` /
  ``skill_match`` types (which skill the router selected);
  ``TOOLS_USED`` covers the legacy ``tool_call_match`` /
  ``parameter_match`` types (the tools invoked and their parameters).
* ``outcome_grounded`` — the 3-tier discrimination of what a
  state-grounded verdict is judging. ``SAFETY`` gates on whether the
  agent left the backend in an unsafe / non-permitted state;
  ``OUTCOME`` checks the end state matches the expected outcome; and
  ``QUALITY`` grades how well-formed the resulting state is once the
  outcome is correct.

The valid (kind, subkind) pairing is enforced by a CHECK constraint:
structured-match subkinds never pair with ``outcome_grounded`` and
vice versa.

EvaluatorUsageResponse.title: EvaluatorUsageResponse
EvaluatorUsageResponse.description: How many evaluation runs currently reference this evaluator.

EvaluatorVersionResponse.title: EvaluatorVersionResponse
EvaluatorVersionResponse.description: Response schema for an evaluator version.

EvictedSnapshotResponse.title: EvictedSnapshotResponse
EvictedSnapshotResponse.description:
Body of the eviction endpoint. Mirrors a subset of
``ProjectSnapshotResponse`` so the frontend can confirm which
snapshot was removed.

ord.title: Ord
ord.description: Zero-based step ordinal within the turn (sorted ASC).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Step kind discriminator: one of 'route', 'tool', 'llm', 'reasoning', or 'model_call'.

input.title: Input

output.title: Output

ExampleAgentStepResponse.title: ExampleAgentStepResponse
ExampleAgentStepResponse.description:
A single agent execution step (route/tool/llm) attached to an ExampleTurn.

Mirrors ``SessionAgentStepResponse``. The dataset side carries an
independent copy of the captured agent steps (``example_agent_steps``
table) so a row's history is preserved even after the source session
is deleted. Shape is identical to the session-side response so the
front-end's ``AgentStepTree`` renderer consumes both interchangeably.

``ord`` is the zero-based intra-turn ordinal — the API guarantees
steps are returned sorted by ``ord ASC`` per turn, so the FE can
iterate without resorting client-side.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Conversational per-turn timestamp — when the turn happened in its source conversation. Sourced from ``ExampleTurn.recorded_at``, distinct from ``created_at`` (the dataset-ingestion time). Populated only for examples bulk-added from a Session; ``null`` for CSV-uploaded, turn-spec, and manually-inserted turns, where the FE renders the role word alone. Mirrors ``SessionTurnResponse.timestamp``.

agent_steps.title: Agent Steps
agent_steps.description: Ordered agent execution steps captured for this turn, sorted by ``ord`` ASC. Empty list (never null) when the turn has no recorded steps. Steps are read-only from the dataset side: they reflect what the source agent actually did at sync time.

ExampleTurnResponse.title: ExampleTurnResponse
ExampleTurnResponse.description:
Wire shape for one ``ExampleTurn`` in a dataset row.

Returned standalone by the per-row turn endpoints (``GET
.../rows/{id}/turns`` etc.) and embedded as the element type of
``DatasetRowResponse.turns`` — the structured per-turn projection
that mirrors the Sessions detail endpoint's ``SessionTurnResponse``.
Field-for-field the dataset-domain analogue of the session shape:
``idx`` / ``role`` / ``content`` / ``timestamp`` plus an ordered
``agent_steps`` array.

Mirrors the grant dialog's two tabs. For ``user`` the ``id`` is the person's email (the engine
keys direct grants on ``lower(email)``); for ``team`` the ``id`` is the team id whose member-set

requested) — WorkOS' ``Invitation`` carries no role on the list read, so pending-list

pass_rate.title: Pass Rate
pass_rate.description: Row pass rate 0-100 for this run

LastEvaluationRun.title: LastEvaluationRun

source.description: Whether this launch grades fresh scenario simulations or already-recorded sessions.

scope.description: 'bulk' creates a named evaluation; 'one-off' is an unnamed evaluation for a single item.

run_only.title: Run Only
run_only.description: Scenarios only: simulate the conversations without grading them.

items.title: Items
items.description: The scenario ids (scenarios-mode) or session ids (sessions-mode) this launch targets.

evaluator_ids.title: Evaluator Ids
evaluator_ids.description: The LLM judges to grade with, in sessions-mode only. Must be absent in scenarios-mode, where evaluators are inherited from each scenario.

agent_version_id.title: Agent Version Id
agent_version_id.description: The connected agent to simulate against, in scenarios-mode only.

name.title: Name
name.description: The evaluation's name, for a bulk launch. Absent for one-off or run-only launches.

view_name.title: View Name
view_name.description: The saved view the items were resolved from, for display. Optional.

tdm_environment_ref.title: Tdm Environment Ref
tdm_environment_ref.description: The test-data environment the launcher selected to run against. When set, it is persisted on the run and activates the environment-scoped lifecycle; when absent the run stays on the unchanged transcript path.

LaunchEvaluationInput.title: LaunchEvaluationInput
LaunchEvaluationInput.description:
The unified launcher's create payload (the locked onConfirm shape).

One typed body backs all four create branches the EvaluationLauncher
modal can emit. Everything is inferred from the entry context the
user opened the modal from; the user assembles almost nothing.

The two orthogonal dimensions:

* ``source`` — ``"scenarios"`` grades a fresh simulation per scenario
  (an agent must run; evaluators are inherited, not picked);
  ``"sessions"`` grades already-recorded sessions (LLM judges are
  picked; nothing is simulated).
* ``scope`` — ``"bulk"`` creates a named evaluation; ``"one-off"`` is
  an unnamed, single-item evaluation homed on the session it ran
  against.

``run_only`` rides on top of scenarios-mode: simulate the
conversations without grading them (a cold-start for a brand-new
scenario that has no observed vocabulary yet).

``items`` are the resolved ids the launch targets — scenario ids when
``source == "scenarios"``, session ids when ``source == "sessions"``.
The FE maps its camelCase ``evaluatorIds`` / ``agent`` /
``viewName`` onto the snake_case fields here, and reduces the picked
connected-agent object to its ``agent_version_id``.

decision.description: Approval decision. Wire values: 'approved' | 'rejected'. 'pending' and 'timeout' are system-only and a 400 is returned.

note.title: Note
note.description: Optional rationale or instructions for the calling agent.

MCPPermissionDecisionInput.title: MCPPermissionDecisionInput
MCPPermissionDecisionInput.description:
Body of ``POST /v1/mcp/permissions/{token}/decision``.

``decision`` reuses the :class:`ApprovalDecision` enum so the same
wire literal (``approved`` / ``rejected``) routes through MCP,
Paddington, and any in-process Strands adapter. Pydantic rejects
other values at the route edge with a 422 before we ever reach
the service.

delivered.title: Delivered
delivered.description: True when the waiter received the decision in time. False means the request had already timed out or been resolved.

MCPPermissionDecisionResponse.title: MCPPermissionDecisionResponse
MCPPermissionDecisionResponse.description:
Compact post-decision response.

The MCP tool call itself returns the executor's full envelope to
the caller of the *tool*; this route's job is just to acknowledge
the decision landed and the waiter was woken. Keeping the response
intentionally narrow avoids leaking permission registry internals
to a client that already has the tool result on its other channel.

member_id.title: Member Id
member_id.description: The subject the access was resolved for (the lowercased identifier).

MemberAccessResponse.title: MemberAccessResponse
MemberAccessResponse.description: The provenance view for one subject — every project/team they can reach and how.

resource_type.title: Resource Type
resource_type.description: Whether the reachable resource is a project or a team.

MemberAccessRow.title: MemberAccessRow
MemberAccessRow.description:
One thing a subject can reach — a project or a team — with the effective role and ``via``.

For a **project**, ``via`` is the design's direct-vs-inherited distinction and ``via_teams`` lists
the display names of the teams the access flows through (empty when the access is ``direct`` only).
For a **team** row (a membership the subject holds), ``via`` is always ``direct`` and ``via_teams``
is empty — you are directly a member of the team. ``name_missing`` flags an unresolved display name
so the UI branches on a bool rather than string-matching the missing-name marker.

MemberTeamChip.description: A team a member belongs to — the Members table's team chips.

``count`` is how many distinct sessions in the project used the tool —
surfaced to the user as a "seen in N sessions" hint so it reads as a

ObservedVocabResponse.description:
The project's learned vocabulary for the expected-output editor.

Mined from the project's observed sessions: the tool names and routes

the observed ``key: value`` param candidates for the requested tool
(scoped to the requested personas when supplied). Cold-start — a
project with no sessions — returns empty arrays so the editor shows

``value`` is the canonical string form of the expected argument —
the same string the observed-vocabulary index surfaces as a
candidate, so what the author pins and what the grader compares are
identical.

workspace_role.title: Workspace Role
workspace_role.description: The requested workspace role, when WorkOS exposes it (often null on list rows).

PendingInviteRow.description:
An OUTSTANDING workspace invitation, surfaced alongside the member roster on the Members tab.

Sourced from WorkOS invitations, filtered to the outstanding lifecycle states (``pending`` and
``expired``) — an accepted invite has become a member and a revoked one is gone, so neither is
surfaced. ``state`` lets the FE pill a row "Invited" vs "Expired" and decide which lifecycle
actions (revoke vs resend) apply. Defined here rather than imported from the invites domain so
the Members assembly stays within its own domain boundary.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor

PendingInvitesResponse.title: PendingInvitesResponse
PendingInvitesResponse.description: A cursor-paginated page of the workspace's pending invitations (newest-first).

PermissionRow.description: One permission row of the matrix, with a verdict per workspace role.

PersonaDraftResponse.description:
Returned from ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/personas/draft``.

chip-style bullets the user can edit individually before saving.
The user remaps these into the JSONB shape the persistable
``CreatePersonaInput`` expects.

The full **Project access** tab payload: every grant on the project with its provenance.

One entry per grant — direct subject grants AND team-mediated grants — exactly as the resolver's
``grants_for_project`` returns them. The UI rolls these up per person (taking the highest role and
collecting each provenance) as the design does; the API surfaces the raw, faithful grant list.

ProjectCounts.title: ProjectCounts

ProjectGrantRow.title: ProjectGrantRow
ProjectGrantRow.description:
One row of the **Project access** tab: a grantee, their role on the project, and *why*.

``via`` is the provenance the design hinges on: ``"direct"`` for a grant made straight to this
grantee, or ``"team:<name>"`` for access *inherited* from a team (the team's resolved display
name). ``name_missing`` is the explicit flag set when a team's display name could not be resolved
(the value then degrades to the package's missing-name marker) so the UI branches on a bool rather
than string-matching.
