ProjectSnapshotResponse.title: ProjectSnapshotResponse
ProjectSnapshotResponse.description:
Snapshot metadata exposed to the API.

The ``payload`` column is intentionally NOT exposed here — it can
be hundreds of kilobytes of mixed-shape rows from across every
domain in scope, and there is no UI for rendering it.

ProviderUsage.title: ProviderUsage
ProviderUsage.description: Per-provider spend + request rollup across the three standard windows.

RecentRunItem.title: RecentRunItem
RecentRunItem.description: Summary of a recent test run.

key.title: Key
key.description: Stable, immutable catalog slug (e.g. ``tone_professional``) — the durable identity an adopted evaluator records as its ``recommended_key``. Opaque adopt + dedupe token; never rendered raw.

description.title: Description
description.description: Plain-language description of what the preset checks

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The judge criteria — a self-contained prose pass/fail (or 1-5) rubric the LLM judge scores the agent's response against. Carries no template placeholders; the judge scaffold binds the output, expected response, and tool calls at scoring time.

kind.description: Top-level taxonomy. Always ``llm_judge`` for recommended presets.

scoring_type.description: How the preset reports its score

pass_threshold.title: Pass Threshold
pass_threshold.description: 1-5-scale pass cutoff. Non-NULL only for the two ``scale_1_5`` presets; ``None`` for the pass/fail presets.

group.title: Group
group.description: Catalog section the preset lists under ("Quality" | "Safety")

method_full.title: Method Full
method_full.description: Full human-readable provenance citation for the chip's tooltip

recommended.title: Recommended
recommended.description: Whether the preset is part of the default-checked starter subset in the adopt modal

rubric.description: Structured form of the preset's ``prompt`` — the pass/fail rules (or 1-5 anchors) plus the non-editable ``lead_in`` framing and the trailing ``guidance`` coda. Adopting a preset copies this onto the new evaluator row, so the adopted check is immediately editable as discrete rule rows. The invariant is byte-exact round-trip: assembling this rubric reproduces ``prompt`` character-for-character.

tailor_first.title: Tailor First
tailor_first.description: Whether an adopted copy of this preset needs per-agent tailoring before it earns its keep. DERIVED from the preset's identity (NOT a stored column): true only for the agent-relative checks whose criteria defer to the user's own agent — its goal, its scope, its authority — and false for the self-contained checks (tone, completeness, grounding, toxicity, bias, injection-resistance, PII). The adopt modal marks the tailor-first presets so the user knows to edit their rules to the agent under test.

RecommendedEvaluator.title: RecommendedEvaluator
RecommendedEvaluator.description:
One preset in the recommended-evaluators starter catalog.

Projected from the immutable ``recommended.RECOMMENDED_EVALUATORS``
tuple — a curated, provenance-tagged set the user browses on
``GET /v1/evaluators/recommended`` and then adopts one at a time
through the ordinary ``POST /v1/evaluators`` create path. The catalog
itself has no table of its own; adoption persists the provenance
(``source = recommended`` + ``recommended_key``) onto the ordinary
evaluator row, so an adopted preset is a fully editable evaluator that
stays recognisable as recommended-origin.

``key`` is the preset's stable, immutable slug (e.g.
``tone_professional``) — the durable identity an adopted evaluator
records as its ``recommended_key``. It is decoupled from the editable
``name`` so the two adopt surfaces dedupe + decouple attach-from-adopt
off the slug, never a name match. It is an OPAQUE token (never
rendered raw on the FE).

Every preset is an ``llm_judge`` (the create path rejects
structured-match creation), so ``kind`` is reused from the create
surface and ``prompt`` is always a self-contained prose rubric the
judge scores against. ``pass_threshold`` is non-NULL only for the
two ``scale_1_5`` presets.

``method`` is a short, friendly provenance chip ("G-Eval", "OWASP",
"Trajectory"); ``method_full`` is the human-readable citation for the
chip's tooltip. These two strings are the ONLY provenance the FE
renders — the raw vendor method token is never sent to the client, so
the no-code surface shows a friendly label and a real citation, never
a bare identifier. ``group`` ("Quality" | "Safety") drives the
section the adopt modal lists the preset under.

RecommendedEvaluatorsResponse.title: RecommendedEvaluatorsResponse
RecommendedEvaluatorsResponse.description:
The recommended-evaluators starter catalog.

A plain ``{items: [...]}`` envelope — NOT the paginated
``PageEnvelope`` the project-scoped evaluators list uses. The catalog
is a fixed, global constant (no pagination, no project scoping, no
DB), so it carries only the items array.

name.title: Name
name.description: Friendly scenario title describing the probe. Never the raw OWASP code.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: What the simulated customer pursues against the agent under test.

owasp_category.title: Owasp Category
owasp_category.description: Friendly label for the OWASP Agentic failure mode this scenario probes (e.g. 'Goal hijack').

owasp_code.title: Owasp Code
owasp_code.description: The OWASP Agentic Top 10 (2026) category code this scenario cites. Footnote metadata, not a display title.

expected_behavior.title: Expected Behavior
expected_behavior.description: Prose guidance on how a safe agent should respond to this scenario.

pairs_with_evaluators.title: Pairs With Evaluators
pairs_with_evaluators.description: Names of recommended evaluators that score this scenario well together.

RecommendedScenario.title: RecommendedScenario
RecommendedScenario.description:
One entry in the read-only recommended-scenarios catalog.

A recommended scenario is a curated adversarial probe a team can
adopt into their own project as a starting point. Each maps to one
OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications (2026) failure mode, surfaced
in the product's own vocabulary: ``name`` is the friendly scenario
title, ``owasp_category`` the friendly category label, and
``owasp_code`` the ``ASI0X`` citation kept only for footnoting the
standard — never as a user-facing title.

RecommendedScenariosResponse.title: RecommendedScenariosResponse
RecommendedScenariosResponse.description:
The full read-only recommended-scenarios catalog.

A plain ``{items: [...]}`` envelope — the catalog is a fixed, small
built-in list, not a paginated tenant resource, so it carries no
page/total scaffolding.

ReconcileAgentVersionsInput.title: ReconcileAgentVersionsInput
ReconcileAgentVersionsInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /agent-versions/reconcile``.

DEV-971 / M14-07. Hides AgentCore agent versions whose
``source_metadata.agent_runtime_arn`` doesn't match the project's
configured runtime. Called from the host save path so the simulator
picker shows only the configured runtime's endpoints.

source.title: Source
source.description: What the original run was driven from.

items.title: Items
items.description: The scenario or session ids the original run targeted.

evaluator_ids.title: Evaluator Ids
evaluator_ids.description: The evaluators the original run graded with.

agent_version_id.title: Agent Version Id
agent_version_id.description: The agent the original run simulated against, if any.

run_only.title: Run Only
run_only.description: Whether the original run simulated without grading.

suggested_name.title: Suggested Name
suggested_name.description: A proposed name for the re-run, derived from the original.

RelaunchSeedResponse.title: RelaunchSeedResponse
RelaunchSeedResponse.description:
Server-resolved seed the launcher pre-fills from on Re-run.

Re-run opens the EvaluationLauncher modal in-place, pre-seeded from
this resolver — it never round-trips a dataset id the way the legacy
``?datasetId=`` route did. Every field mirrors the launcher's own
payload so the modal can rehydrate its two dimensions and its picked
values directly.

``items`` are the ids the original run targeted (scenario ids for a
scenario-sourced run). ``evaluator_ids`` is the inherited/selected
judge set. ``agent_version_id`` is the pinned agent (scenario runs).
``run_only`` echoes whether the original run suppressed grading.
``suggested_name`` is a fresh name proposal derived from the original
run's name so the re-run doesn't collide titles.

member_id.title: Member Id
member_id.description: The removed member's WorkOS user id.

grants_revoked.title: Grants Revoked
grants_revoked.description: How many of the member's direct project grants were revoked by the cascade.

RemoveMemberResponse.title: RemoveMemberResponse
RemoveMemberResponse.description:
Result of removing a workspace member: the removed member + how many direct grants were swept.

``grants_revoked`` is the number of direct project grants the removed member held that were
cascaded away (spec D4) — surfaced so the UI can confirm the cleanup ("Removed · revoked N project
grants"). Team-mediated grants are not part of the sweep (removing a member doesn't dissolve a
team), so they are not counted here.

RenameEvaluationRunInput.title: RenameEvaluationRunInput
RenameEvaluationRunInput.description:
Body for ``PATCH /evaluations/{run_id}`` — persists a run rename.

The Run Detail header ships an inline-editable run title so a user
can label a finished run ("baseline", "post-prompt-tweak"). This is
the wire shape that title's commit handler sends.

``name`` carries the same ``1..255`` bound as the underlying
``evaluation_runs.name`` column. The ``<InlineEditableTitle>``
primitive trims client-side, but the server re-validates: the
``before``-mode field validator strips surrounding whitespace, so a
whitespace-only payload (``"   "``) collapses to ``""`` and is
rejected by ``min_length`` as a 422 rather than silently persisted
as a blank run title. An oversized name is likewise rejected before
it reaches the DB.

RenameTeamInput.title: RenameTeamInput
RenameTeamInput.description: Body for ``PATCH /teams/{id}`` — rename a team (display-name only; never a membership change).

ReorderDatasetColumnsInput.title: ReorderDatasetColumnsInput
ReorderDatasetColumnsInput.description:
Body for ``POST /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns/reorder``.

``keys`` is the desired display order for the dataset's columns.
The service rejects requests whose ``keys`` aren't a strict
permutation of the dataset's current column keys — adds and removes
must go through the create/delete endpoints so version-bump events
stay clean.

ReorderSessionAnnotationColumnsInput.title: ReorderSessionAnnotationColumnsInput
ReorderSessionAnnotationColumnsInput.description:
Body for ``POST .../sessions/annotation-columns/reorder``.

``keys`` is the desired display order. The service rejects requests
whose ``keys`` aren't a strict permutation of the project's current
column keys — adds / removes go through create / delete.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

RequirementListResponse.title: RequirementListResponse
RequirementListResponse.description:
Paginated list of requirements.

Extends the shared :class:`PageEnvelope` so the response carries
``items`` / ``page`` / ``page_size`` / ``total`` / ``total_pages``
by name — the same wire shape as ``SessionListResponse``. The
sidebar count badge reads ``total`` (with ``limit: 1``); until the
Requirements surface ships, ``total`` is always ``0``.

RequirementResponse.title: RequirementResponse
RequirementResponse.description:
A single requirement.

Placeholder shape — the Requirements workflow surface is not built
yet, so the list endpoint never emits one of these. The fields
mirror the common metadata every project-scoped row carries so the
real domain can adopt this schema unchanged when it lands.

ResolveSavedViewResponse.title: ResolveSavedViewResponse
ResolveSavedViewResponse.description:
Response for ``POST /saved-views/{view_id}/resolve``.

Resolves a saved view to the concrete items it currently selects,
using SERVER time for any date-window filter. ``item_ids`` are
opaque machine handles the launcher passes straight through to a
launch payload — never rendered; the launcher shows the view NAME.

``kind`` is the resolved item domain (``sessions`` | ``scenarios``),
derived from the persisted view ``kind``.

ResultVerdict.title: ResultVerdict
ResultVerdict.description:
The three-tier state-grounded verdict for one result (and aggregated per run).

Safety / Outcome / Quality are the AE-3 ``outcome_grounded`` evaluator
subkinds — the three-tier discrimination of what a state-grounded verdict
is judging: Safety gates on whether the agent left the backend in an
unsafe state, Outcome on whether the end state matches the expected
outcome, Quality on how well-formed the resulting state is. Each tier is
``pass`` / ``fail`` / ``neutral``; ``neutral`` when the run pinned no
evaluator for that tier (it was never graded) or when the row was an
env-problem (never scored). Derived read-side from the evaluator taxonomy
applied to the row's scores — no column, no migration backs it.

ResultVerdictMatrix.title: ResultVerdictMatrix
ResultVerdictMatrix.description:
The five-dimension matrix the run-detail ``verdict-matrix`` primitive renders.

The locked column set, in locked order: Safety / Outcome / Quality /
Behavior / End-state (matching the FE primitive's
``VERDICT_MATRIX_DIMENSIONS``). Safety / Outcome / Quality are the
three ``outcome_grounded`` tiers; Behavior is the existing transcript
dimension (``llm_judge`` + ``structured_match`` scores); End-state is the
state-grounded read-back over the captured backend snapshot.

``end_state`` degrades to ``neutral`` ("not graded") whenever no backend
snapshot was captured for the row — ``capture_state`` is not yet wired
(RFC 0013 §5), so for now End-state is ``neutral`` on every run without a
captured snapshot. That is expected; it must never surface as a red fail.

``env_problem`` marks the denominator-excluded collapse: a row whose
runtime was blocked or whose environment failed was never scored, so
every dimension reads ``neutral`` and ``env_problem`` is ``True`` — the
FE collapses such a row into the single neutral "Environment problem —
not the agent" affordance, never a fail, never counted toward the score.

description.title: Description
description.description: What the role can do, in plain language.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Whether the role is workspace-wide or project-scoped (guest).

RoleCard.title: RoleCard
RoleCard.description: One role card on the Roles & permissions tab.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: The workspace role cards, in catalog order.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: The permission matrix rows, derived from the trustai_core.authz catalog.

RolesAndPermissionsResponse.title: RolesAndPermissionsResponse
RolesAndPermissionsResponse.description: The full Roles & permissions payload: role cards + the catalog-derived permission matrix.

RowAnnotationsResponse.title: RowAnnotationsResponse
RowAnnotationsResponse.description:
Wire shape for ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows/{row_id}/annotations``.

``values`` is a ``{column_key: value}`` map. Orphaned keys (keys
whose ``DatasetColumn`` was deleted) are filtered out on read so
callers never see schema-orphan junk; the values still live in
Postgres (per the M3-06 EDD's orphan tolerance) so re-creating the
column resurrects them.

RuntimeObservability.title: RuntimeObservability
RuntimeObservability.description:
Runtime-observability read-model for one result: stats + ordered timeline.

Feeds the result fly-in's Runtime region (Surface 4b's TIMELINE tab) — the
``runtime-timeline`` primitive's stat tiles + chronological event list.
Derived read-side from the result's frozen ``example_snapshot.agent_steps[]``
(RFC 0003 §4.2) — a DIFFERENT source than the end-state diff / change ledger
(which read the per-score backend snapshots); no column, no migration backs
it.

A result with no ``agent_steps`` degrades to the empty shape — zeroed/None
stats and ``events == []`` — the standard "never None, return []"
convention the FE renders as "No runtime events recorded." The
``guardrail`` / ``usage`` legs are commonly empty even when tool steps are
present (their writers fire only on a block / on usage metadata); that is
graceful-empty, not an error. The observability PRODUCER (backend M3 FR-18,
the per-step ``elapsed_ms`` / richer telemetry writer) is parked and out of
scope — this is the READ/projection only, which lights up unchanged when the
producer lands.

RuntimeStats.title: RuntimeStats
RuntimeStats.description:
Aggregate runtime stat tiles for one result (latency / tokens / guardrail).

Feeds the result fly-in's Runtime region (Surface 4b's TIMELINE tab) — the
three stat tiles the ``runtime-timeline`` primitive renders above the event
list. Derived read-side from the result's frozen
``example_snapshot.agent_steps[]`` (RFC 0003 §4.2); no column, no migration
backs it.

The read-model carries RAW aggregates, not formatted strings — the
primitive's contract is that locale / precision / units ("2.4 s",
"1,240 in · 320 out") live at the call site, so the FE formats these.
``total_latency_ms`` sums every step's recorded ``elapsed_ms`` and is ``0``
when no step recorded a timing (the runtime producer, backend M3 FR-18, is
parked). ``input_tokens`` / ``output_tokens`` / ``total_tokens`` sum across
the ``usage`` steps and are ``None`` (not ``0``) when no usage step recorded
that field, so the FE distinguishes "no token telemetry" from "zero
tokens". ``guardrail_block_count`` counts the ``guardrail`` steps that
recorded a block.

RuntimeTimelineEvent.title: RuntimeTimelineEvent
RuntimeTimelineEvent.description:
One ordered runtime event: a tool call, a guardrail block, or a usage marker.

Feeds one row of the ``runtime-timeline`` primitive's chronological event
list. ``kind`` is the locked event vocabulary the FE keys its icon + tone
off (a ``guardrail`` event reads ``danger``, a ``tool`` / ``usage`` event
reads ``neutral``). ``label`` is the human event label — the tool name for
a tool call, a fixed phrase for a guardrail / usage event; NEVER a raw UUID
(AGENTS.md "Built for Humans and Agents"). ``detail`` is an optional
one-line elaboration. ``latency_ms`` is the step's recorded timing
(``None`` when no ``elapsed_ms`` was written — FR-18 parked). ``total_tokens``
is the token total for a usage event (``None`` for non-usage events).

SavedViewCreate.title: SavedViewCreate
SavedViewCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /saved-views``.

Server stamps ``created_by`` from the authenticated user and ignores
any client-supplied ``builtin`` flag (always created as ``False``).

SavedViewDefaultResponse.title: SavedViewDefaultResponse
SavedViewDefaultResponse.description:
Response for ``POST /saved-views/{view_id}/default``.

Reports the post-toggle state: ``is_user_default`` is ``True`` when
the view is now the calling user's default, ``False`` when the call
cleared it.

SavedViewResponse.title: SavedViewResponse
SavedViewResponse.description:
Single SavedView serialized for HTTP responses.

``is_user_default`` is a per-request, per-user flag — it is not a
column on the SavedView. The service derives it from a LEFT JOIN
against ``saved_view_user_defaults`` for the authenticated user, so
the same SavedView serializes ``True`` for one user and ``False``
for another.

``kind`` serializes the persisted storage kind verbatim — there is no
read aliasing between surfaces.

SavedViewUpdate.title: SavedViewUpdate
SavedViewUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /saved-views/{id}``.

Both fields are optional. ``created_by`` is immutable (audit-only)
and ``builtin`` cannot be flipped from the client.

5.description: What a 5 (top of the scale) looks like

3.description: What a 3 (the pass-bar midpoint) looks like

1.description: What a 1 (bottom of the scale) looks like

ScaleAnchors.title: ScaleAnchors
ScaleAnchors.description:
The three pinned anchors of a 1-5-scale rubric.

Each anchor is the clause that completes a ``Rate N when …`` line in the
assembled criteria prose: ``high`` -> ``Rate 5 when {high}``, ``mid`` ->
``Rate 3 when {mid}``, ``low`` -> ``Rate 1 when {low}``. The wire shape
is keyed by the literal scale points (``"5"`` / ``"3"`` / ``"1"``) so the
JSON a client sends reads as the scale it describes.

evaluation_run_count.title: Evaluation Run Count
evaluation_run_count.description: How many evaluation runs reference this scenario. While non-zero the delete is blocked — those runs would be orphaned.

affected_session_count.title: Affected Session Count
affected_session_count.description: How many sessions this scenario produced. They survive the delete (their link is cleared, their snapshot kept) — this only warns.

can_delete.title: Can Delete
can_delete.description: Whether the scenario can be deleted now. False when evaluation history references it.

ScenarioDeleteCheck.title: ScenarioDeleteCheck
ScenarioDeleteCheck.description:
Pre-delete impact signals for one scenario, read before confirming.

The delete UX reads this to warn (or block) the user honestly before
they confirm — the two relationships that change a scenario delete:

* The evaluation-run reference count is the BLOCKER. Evaluation
  history references a scenario through a no-cascade foreign key, so
  the delete is refused with a 409 while this is non-zero. The
  DELETE endpoint enforces the same gate server-side; this read just
  lets the UX surface the consequence before the user commits.
* The affected-session count is a WARNING, not a blocker. The
  sessions a scenario produced are linked with ``ON DELETE SET
  NULL`` and carry a frozen point-in-time snapshot, so they survive
  the delete intact — only the live back-link is cleared. The UX
  warns that this many runs will be unlinked, but the delete still
  proceeds.

``can_delete`` mirrors the server-side gate exactly (it is ``True``
when, and only when, the evaluation-run count is zero) so the UX can
enable or disable the confirm control without re-deriving the rule.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

ScenarioListResponse.title: ScenarioListResponse
ScenarioListResponse.description: Paginated list of flat, project-scoped scenarios.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-authored persona name shown on the pill.

ScenarioPersona.title: ScenarioPersona
ScenarioPersona.description:
One persona attached to a scenario — id plus human-authored name.

The ``/scenarios`` table renders attached personas as removable
name pills, so the read shape carries the resolved name alongside
the id rather than forcing the frontend to resolve every id against
a separate roster fetch. The raw id stays on the wire only so the
pill's remove action can target it; the user never sees it.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Identifier of the project this scenario belongs to.

display_id.title: Display Id
display_id.description: Per-project counter for this scenario. An internal ordering surface — clients render the human-readable label below instead of this raw number.

dataset_id.title: Dataset Id
dataset_id.description: Identifier of the storage parent this scenario's row belongs to.

name.title: Name
name.description: Short label for the scenario. May be empty for a scenario captured before a name was set.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: Free-text objective the simulated user pursues in this scenario. Empty until an author sets one.

personas.title: Personas
personas.description: Personas attached to this scenario, in selection order. Each fans out into its own conversation on a run.

persona_ids.title: Persona Ids
persona_ids.description: Identifiers of the attached personas, in selection order.

persona_names.title: Persona Names
persona_names.description: Human-authored names of the attached personas, in selection order.

expected_routes.title: Expected Routes
expected_routes.description: Routes this scenario is expected to take. Empty until expected output is authored.

expected_tools.title: Expected Tools
expected_tools.description: Actions this scenario is expected to call. Empty until expected output is authored.

evaluator_ids.title: Evaluator Ids
evaluator_ids.description: Identifiers of the evaluators attached to this scenario, in attach order.

evaluator_names.title: Evaluator Names
evaluator_names.description: Human-authored names of the attached evaluators, paired index-for-index with the ids. The Evaluators column renders these as chips.

session_ids.title: Session Ids
session_ids.description: Identifiers of the sessions produced by running this scenario, most recent first.

session_count.title: Session Count
session_count.description: How many times this scenario has been run and scored.

has_test_data.title: Has Test Data
has_test_data.description: Whether this scenario carries Test Data — at least one authored starting-data or expected-end-state record. A run over a Test-Data-carrying scenario must plant that data into a connected Environment before the agent starts, so the evaluation launcher's Environment step is required for it; a scenario with none grades the agent's transcript directly and leaves the Environment optional. Populated on the list AND single-scenario reads from the scenario test-records store; False when the scenario has no authored Test Data (the concurrency-only envelope, with no records, does not count — there is nothing to plant).

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Tags applied to this scenario for grouping and filtering.

created_by_display.title: Created By Display
created_by_display.description: Name or email of the person who created this scenario. Empty when the author cannot be resolved.

fanout_conversation_count.title: Fanout Conversation Count
fanout_conversation_count.description: How many conversations one run of this scenario produces — one per attached persona, at least one.

display_label.title: Display Label
display_label.description:
Human-readable scenario handle.

Renders the per-project ``display_id`` counter as
``SCN-{display_id:06d}`` — six zero-padded digits. The flat
``/scenarios`` facade shows this everywhere a scenario is named for
a non-technical user, so they reference a stable handle instead of
the row UUID (kept on ``id`` for deep links / FK integrity) or the
old render-only positional index.

ScenarioResponse.title: ScenarioResponse
ScenarioResponse.description:
Flat, project-scoped read shape for one Scenario.

A Scenario stores the SPEC a simulated user pursues — a goal, the
personas that fan it out, and (in a later wave) the expected routes
and actions it should produce. It is one row of the re-rooted
``dataset_rows`` storage, surfaced here in the product's vocabulary
with no scenario-set container around it.

ScoreHistoryEntry.title: ScoreHistoryEntry
ScoreHistoryEntry.description: A single score history data point.

ScoreHistoryListResponse.title: ScoreHistoryListResponse
ScoreHistoryListResponse.description: Paginated list of score history entries for sparkline charts.

run_count.title: Run Count
run_count.description: Number of completed runs that day

ScoreHistoryPoint.title: ScoreHistoryPoint
ScoreHistoryPoint.description: A single data point in the score history timeline.

ScoringType.title: ScoringType
ScoringType.description: How an evaluator reports its score.

kind.description: Which sub-app surface this hit points at.

id.title: Id
id.description: The entity's stable id (a UUID for every AE surface).

title.title: Title
title.description: Primary palette line — the entity's human-facing name.

subtitle.title: Subtitle
subtitle.description: Secondary palette line — a short humanised descriptor (e.g. 'v3 · 142 rows').

deep_link.title: Deep Link
deep_link.description: App-relative path to the entity's detail (or nearest list) surface.

SearchHit.title: SearchHit
SearchHit.description:
A single typed search hit, ready to render as a ⌘K palette row.

Every field is server-built and renders verbatim in the Nexus
palette — ``title`` / ``subtitle`` carry humanised copy (never a raw
enum literal or bare UUID), and ``deep_link`` is an app-relative path
the shell prefixes with the AE mount point to navigate.

SearchHitKind.title: SearchHitKind
SearchHitKind.description:
The user-facing entity a search hit points at.

One value per sub-app surface the global palette can deep-link into.
``requirement`` is part of the contract for uniformity with the other
table surfaces even though AE's Requirements workflow has no backing
table yet — the searcher simply returns zero requirement hits today,
and the kind is reserved so the wire shape never changes when the
surface ships.

clipped.title: Clipped
clipped.description: True when matches were dropped to honour the result cap — the query was too broad.

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description:
The merged, capped result set for one search query.

``hits`` is ordered deterministically (by kind in workflow order,
then title) so a repeated query returns a stable palette. ``clipped``
is ``True`` when the unbounded match set exceeded ``limit`` and the
tail was dropped — the palette reads it to show a "refine your search"
affordance instead of implying these were the only matches.

workspace_role.title: Workspace Role
workspace_role.description: The workspace role to grant on acceptance (admin | member | guest).

SendInviteInput.title: SendInviteInput
SendInviteInput.description: Invite an email to the caller's workspace with a workspace role.

ord.title: Ord
ord.description: Zero-based step ordinal within the turn (sorted ASC).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Step kind discriminator: one of 'route', 'tool', 'llm', 'reasoning', or 'model_call'.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label — route name, tool name, or model name.

input.title: Input
input.description: Kind-specific input payload (params for tools, prompt for llm). Nullable.

output.title: Output
output.description: Kind-specific output payload (response for tools, completion for llm). Nullable.

SessionAgentStepResponse.title: SessionAgentStepResponse
SessionAgentStepResponse.description:
A single agent execution step within a turn (M2-02 / DEV-637, wired by DEV-733).

Mirrors a row in the ``session_agent_steps`` table. Shape matches what
the front-end ``AgentStepTree`` renderer at
``apps/web/src/components/features/sessions/agent-step-tree.tsx``
consumes: a discriminator ``kind`` ∈ {route, tool, llm, reasoning,
model_call}, a
human-readable ``name``, and optional ``input`` / ``output`` blobs the
expandable tool-call rows render as syntax-highlighted JSON.

``ord`` is the zero-based intra-turn ordinal — the API guarantees
steps are returned sorted by ``ord ASC`` per turn, so the FE can
iterate without resorting client-side.

SessionAnnotationColumnResponse.title: SessionAnnotationColumnResponse
SessionAnnotationColumnResponse.description:
Wire shape for a SessionAnnotationColumn.

``config`` is returned as the raw dict — clients dispatch on ``type``
and read the type-specific keys. The server validated the shape at
write time.

SessionAnnotationValueResponse.title: SessionAnnotationValueResponse
SessionAnnotationValueResponse.description:
Wire shape for ``PUT /v1/sessions/{session_id}/annotations/{column_key}``.

Returns the freshly persisted cell. ``value`` is typed by the parent
column's ``type`` (``null`` when the cell was cleared).

result_id.title: Result Id
result_id.description: EvaluationResult id — a machine handle for deep-linking; never rendered raw.

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: EvaluationRun id — a machine handle for the ``/evaluations/{run_id}`` deep link; never rendered raw. Null for an ephemeral run-less one-off evaluation (homed on the session, with no enclosing run to link to).

run_name.title: Run Name
run_name.description: Human display name of the evaluation run this result belongs to.

persona_label.title: Persona Label
persona_label.description: Humanised persona this result was scored against — the persona's frozen snapshot name, or 'Default persona' for a personaless row. Never a raw ``persona_id`` UUID.

passed.title: Passed
passed.description: Whether this result passed every evaluator (the result's ``passed_all`` flag).

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.description: Structured row outcome beyond pass/fail — a denominator-excluded class that is neither a pass nor a fail. ``'runtime_blocked'`` when the runtime's guardrail suppressed the response; ``'infra_failure'`` when the supporting infrastructure failed before the agent could answer; ``'harness_failure'`` when the evaluation harness was misconfigured for the row. No scores exist for any of these; ``null`` for an ordinary scored row.

evaluated_at.title: Evaluated At
evaluated_at.description: When this evaluation result was recorded.

scores.title: Scores
scores.description: Per-evaluator verdicts for this result. Empty (never null) for a runtime-blocked row, where the judge never ran.

SessionEvaluationProjection.title: SessionEvaluationProjection
SessionEvaluationProjection.description:
One automated evaluation a scenario-simulated session was scored in.

A scenario-generated ("From eval") session carries the evaluation grain
that produced it in ``source_metadata``; this projection reverse-links
that grain to the ``EvaluationResult`` row(s) the session's conversation
was scored against, so the session fly-in can render the evaluation
outcome inline. A read-time projection — there is no ORM relationship
and no stored column.

Built for humans: ``run_name`` and ``persona_label`` are resolved
display strings; ``result_id`` / ``run_id`` are machine handles the FE
deep-links from but never renders raw.

evaluator_name.title: Evaluator Name
evaluator_name.description: Resolved display name of the evaluator that produced this verdict. Always a human string — the raw ``evaluator_id`` UUID is never surfaced as a label.

score.title: Score
score.description: Numeric evaluator score in [0, 1], or null when the evaluator emits none.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: Evaluator's free-text rationale for the verdict, when present.

SessionEvaluationScoreProjection.title: SessionEvaluationScoreProjection
SessionEvaluationScoreProjection.description:
One evaluator's verdict on the automated evaluation of a session.

The session-side mirror of one ``EvaluationScore`` row. The session
fly-in's evaluation card paints a per-evaluator pass/fail line, so the
projection carries the resolved human ``evaluator_name`` (never the raw
``evaluator_id`` UUID, per AGENTS.md "Built for Humans and Agents")
alongside the machine ``verdict`` token and the numeric ``score``.

routed_to.title: Routed To
routed_to.description: Distinct ``route``-kind step names across all sessions in the project. Sourced from ``session_agent_steps`` rows where ``kind = 'route'``. Sorted alphabetically.

tools_called.title: Tools Called
tools_called.description: Distinct ``tool``-kind step names across all sessions in the project. Sourced from ``session_agent_steps`` rows where ``kind = 'tool'``. Sorted alphabetically.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Distinct tag values across all sessions in the project. Same data as ``GET .../tag-vocabulary``, surfaced here so the filter popover can populate every dimension in one fetch.

SessionFilterVocabularyResponse.title: SessionFilterVocabularyResponse
SessionFilterVocabularyResponse.description:
Distinct vocabulary for every supported Sessions filter dimension.

DEV-752 (M8-39). Powers the Sessions filter popover's level-2
checkbox lists. One round-trip populates every dimension at once
(Routed To, Tools Called, Tags). Each list is alphabetically sorted
server-side so the popover renders a stable order without
re-sorting client-side. Date is omitted — that dimension uses a
range picker, not a vocabulary.

All lists are project-scoped; no values from other projects leak in.
Empty lists (never null) are returned for projects with no matching
data so the popover can render an explicit "no values yet" state
without nullish-coalescing.

total_session_count.title: Total Session Count
total_session_count.description: Total Session rows in this project at response build time (DEV-1013). ``None`` for in-flight imports; populated once the import has completed or failed.

SessionImportResponse.title: SessionImportResponse
SessionImportResponse.description:
Response schema for a session import job.

DEV-1013: ``total_session_count`` is the project's Session row
count when the import finished (computed at response build time —
not stored). The Sync-result dialog reads this so it can render
"Project already has N sessions" on an empty-window run instead of
a bare ``Imported 0 sessions`` line that reads as a failure.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

SessionListResponse.title: SessionListResponse
SessionListResponse.description:
Paginated list of sessions.

DEV-750 (M8-37). Extends the shared :class:`PageEnvelope` so the FE
generic ``useTablePagination`` hook reads the metadata fields
(``page``, ``page_size``, ``total``, ``total_pages``) by name. The
``items`` field is re-declared with the concrete element type so
the generated OpenAPI schema documents a typed list.

SessionMessageInput.title: SessionMessageInput
SessionMessageInput.description: A single message in a conversation session.

display_id.title: Display Id
display_id.description: Per-project monotonic counter (DEV-741 / M8-28). Internal ordering surface — clients render the human-readable ``display_label`` instead of this raw integer.

end_user_id.title: End User Id
end_user_id.description: External end-user identifier from the customer's source system (DEV-744 / M8-31). For Bedrock-synced sessions this is the AgentCore actor ID. ``null`` for pre-DEV-744 rows and any source that doesn't carry one.

routed_to.title: Routed To
routed_to.description: Distinct ``route``-kind agent-step names for this session. Sourced from ``session_agent_steps`` rows where ``kind = 'route'`` and sorted alphabetically. Powers the default-visible "Routed To" column on the Sessions list. Empty list (never null) when the session recorded no routing steps — the same per-row projection of the data ``GET .../filter-vocabulary`` aggregates project-wide.

tools_called.title: Tools Called
tools_called.description: Distinct ``tool``-kind agent-step names for this session. Sourced from ``session_agent_steps`` rows where ``kind = 'tool'`` and sorted alphabetically. Powers the default-visible "Tools Called" column on the Sessions list. Empty list (never null) when the session called no tools — the same per-row projection of the data ``GET .../filter-vocabulary`` aggregates project-wide.

origin_kind.title: Origin Kind
origin_kind.description: Provenance discriminator (DEV-765 / M11-02, surfaced on the response by DEV-778). Tells the FE where this session came from so it can render an origin badge / column without re-querying the filtered list. Backed by the ``ck_sessions_origin_kind`` CHECK constraint at the DB layer.

simulation_id.title: Simulation Id
simulation_id.description: Back-pointer to the simulation that emitted this session (DEV-765 / M11-02, exposed on response by DEV-778). ``None`` for every non-simulator origin and for simulator-emitted sessions whose source simulation has since been deleted (``ON DELETE SET NULL`` at the DB layer).

source.title: Source
source.description: Where this session came from, as the scenario-aware provenance discriminator the Sessions list renders as a Source chip. A read-time projection over (scenario_id, origin_kind) — NOT a stored column. Precedence: a present scenario link wins (``scenario`` regardless of origin_kind); otherwise a playground capture maps to ``playground``; everything else (synced / uploaded / external ingest) maps to ``external``. This is a machine discriminator — the FE Source chip maps it to the locked title-case label (Scenario / Playground / External). The raw ``origin_kind`` token (synced / uploaded / simulator) is NEVER surfaced as the source value.

scenario_id.title: Scenario Id
scenario_id.description: The scenario this session was simulated from (GENERATED FROM provenance), or ``null`` for organic / playground / legacy sessions. Stamped at generation time; never backfilled. A present value makes ``source`` resolve to ``scenario``.

persona_id.title: Persona Id
persona_id.description: The persona this session was simulated against (GENERATED FROM provenance), or ``null`` when the session was not scenario-generated or ran with no persona. Stamped at generation time; never backfilled.

session_snapshot.title: Session Snapshot
session_snapshot.description: Point-in-time snapshot captured when a scenario-generated session was created, so the fly-in 'Simulated from' cards render the historical scenario/persona values even after the live entity is edited or deleted. Shape: ``{capturedAt, model, scenario:{id,goal}, persona:{id,name,description,systemPrompt}}``. Expected routes/tools are deliberately excluded — those are grading concerns on the Evaluation, not the session. ``null`` for organic / playground / legacy sessions.

model.title: Model
model.description: Human-facing Model / agent-build descriptor this session ran against. A read-time projection (no stored column on the session, no migration): resolved from the pinned agent version — its synced ``model`` / ``model_id`` metadata when present, otherwise the operator-chosen build label — and falling back to the frozen ``session_snapshot`` model for a scenario-generated session whose agent version was later deleted (``ON DELETE SET NULL``). ``null`` for organic / playground / legacy sessions that carry neither. Never a raw provider kind or UUID — the Details tab renders it verbatim.

evaluated.title: Evaluated
evaluated.description: Whether this session has been scored by at least one automated evaluation — i.e. ``evaluations`` is non-empty. ``False`` for every organic / playground / legacy session and for a scenario-simulated session whose grain resolves to no EvaluationResult. A read-time projection (no stored column, no migration); populated only on the single-session detail read (``GET /sessions/{id}``), where the fly-in renders the evaluation card. Always ``False`` on the list paths, which do not project it.

passed_all.title: Passed All
passed_all.description: Whether EVERY automated evaluation of this session passed. Derived from ``evaluations`` — ``True`` only when ``evaluated`` is ``True`` and every non-blocked result's ``passed`` flag is set. ``False`` when the session is unevaluated (consistent with ``evaluated=False``) or any evaluation failed. A read-time projection; populated on the detail read only.

evaluations.title: Evaluations
evaluations.description: Automated evaluations this session was scored in, reverse-linked from the evaluation grain the scenario-simulated session carries in ``source_metadata``. Empty (never null) for unevaluated sessions and on the list paths, which do not project it — populated only on ``GET /sessions/{id}``. A read-time projection: no ORM relationship, no stored column, no migration.

duration_ms.title: Duration Ms
duration_ms.description: Elapsed conversation time in milliseconds, computed as ``MAX(session_turns.created_at) - MIN(session_turns.created_at)`` for the session. This is a pure computed projection — not a stored column, so no migration backs it. ``null`` for sessions with fewer than 2 turns, where no meaningful elapsed time exists. Powers the Sessions table ``Duration`` column.

turns.title: Turns
turns.description: Structured per-turn rows from the session_turns table (M2-01 / DEV-635). Populated only on GET /sessions/{id} when ?include=turns is set; ``null`` everywhere else for wire-compat with the legacy ``messages`` shape. After M7-04 / DEV-687 dropped the legacy ``sessions.messages`` JSON column, ``session_turns`` is the source of truth — ``messages`` is kept on the response as a back-compat projection until the date advertised by the ``Deprecation`` response header.

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.description: The reviewer's schema-driven annotation values for this session, as a ``{column_key: value}`` map keyed by the project's annotation columns. Each value is typed by its column's ``type`` (bool / number / string / string-list). Schema-orphan keys (whose column was deleted from the project) and cleared (null) cells are filtered out, so the map only carries live, entered values — ``{}`` for an un-annotated session. Populated on the list + detail read paths (batched, no N+1); empty on create / ingest responses. The session table cell and the fly-in panel both read from this single source.

annotation_columns.title: Annotation Columns
annotation_columns.description: The project's annotation schema — the typed columns reviewers fill in against every session in the project, in display order. The same schema for every session in a project list; carried on the response so the table header and the fly-in panel render the columns without a second round-trip. Populated on the list + detail read paths (batched per project, no N+1); empty on create / ingest responses.

turn_count.title: Turn Count
turn_count.description: Number of turns in the conversation.

display_label.title: Display Label
display_label.description:
Human-readable session label, origin-aware.

Renders the per-project ``display_id`` counter through the canonical
formatter: a session we generated in-product (simulator / eval /
playground / scenario, ``origin_kind == "simulator"``) reads
``RUN-{display_id:06d}``; an externally-synced session (gateway /
CloudWatch / upload) reads ``SES-{display_id:06d}`` — six zero-padded
digits either way, off the same shared counter. The UI uses this
everywhere a session is shown to a non-technical user (Sessions table
cell, side-panel header, etc.) so they never see the raw UUID. UUIDs
remain available on ``id`` for FK integrity / deep links, and either
prefix form still resolves to the same session.

events_total_estimate.title: Events Total Estimate
events_total_estimate.description: Best-effort projection of total events for this connection. Null when the connector has no actor inventory yet.

actor_count.title: Actor Count
actor_count.description: AgentCore actors visible on this batch.

batch_index.title: Batch Index
batch_index.description: Zero-based batch index, echoed from the request.

SessionSyncBatchDiagnostics.title: SessionSyncBatchDiagnostics
SessionSyncBatchDiagnostics.description:
Non-secret per-batch diagnostics surfaced by the sync-batch endpoint.

Mirrors the gateway diagnostics shape so the FE can render counters
without parsing nested envelopes.

host_id.title: Host Id
host_id.description: Host the FE wants to advance. The first call may omit this; the response then names the next host the loop should resume.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.description: Resume cursor returned by the previous batch, or null on the first call.

batch_index.title: Batch Index
batch_index.description: Zero-based index of this batch within the loop. Echoed onto diagnostics for progress UIs.

SessionSyncBatchInput.title: SessionSyncBatchInput
SessionSyncBatchInput.description:
Request body for one batch of a checkpoint-driven Bedrock sync.

The FE drives the loop: each call advances one host one batch and the
response carries the resume cursor plus diagnostics needed for the
progress indicator. Omit every field on the first call.

synced.title: Synced
synced.description: Sessions newly persisted by this batch.

skipped.title: Skipped
skipped.description: Sessions skipped by this batch (already present).

total_session_count.title: Total Session Count
total_session_count.description: Total Session rows in the project after this batch.

host_name.title: Host Name
host_name.description: Display name of the advanced host.

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: Whether the loop should continue. True when this host has more batches OR there are additional unprocessed Bedrock hosts on the project.

next_host_id.title: Next Host Id
next_host_id.description: When ``has_more`` is true and the current host completed, the host the next batch should advance.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.description: Resume cursor for the next batch on this host. Null when this host completed.

SessionSyncBatchResponse.title: SessionSyncBatchResponse
SessionSyncBatchResponse.description: Response for one batch of a checkpoint-driven Bedrock sync.

host_id.title: Host Id
host_id.description: Connected-agent host to sync. When set, only this host is synced (single-target). When null, every syncable host on the project is walked (legacy project-wide behaviour).

SessionSyncInput.title: SessionSyncInput
SessionSyncInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/sync``.

The Sessions header "Sync now" control is a per-agent dropdown: the
user picks exactly one connected agent and only that agent's host is
synced. ``host_id`` names that single target. When omitted (legacy
callers / no body), the sync falls back to walking every syncable
host on the project — the prior project-wide behaviour.

total_session_count.title: Total Session Count
total_session_count.description: Total Session rows in this project after the sync completed (DEV-1013). Computed at response time — not stored. Lets the FE distinguish a genuinely empty project from an empty-window result on a project that already has data.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Human-readable per-host error messages. Empty on full success.

SessionSyncResponse.title: SessionSyncResponse
SessionSyncResponse.description:
Response from ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/sync``.

DEV-708 (M9-02). Bedrock session sync runs synchronously per host
and reports an aggregate count of newly persisted sessions plus the
count of duplicates skipped by the dedup check. Per-host errors are
captured in ``errors`` so a partial-success response is still useful
— a misconfigured AgentCore host doesn't block another host's sync.

DEV-1013: ``total_session_count`` reports the project's total
Session row count after the sync completed. The "Sync complete /
New: 0 / Already in TrustAI: 0" empty-window result confused users
when the project already had 26 sessions — the per-run ``skipped``
counter only reflects duplicates seen in *this* run. The total lets
the dialog say "Project already has N sessions" instead of bare
zeros.

id.title: Id
id.description: Host id — passed back as the sync target; never rendered.

system_name.title: System Name
system_name.description: Human-readable source-system name (the dropdown group label).

SessionSyncTarget.title: SessionSyncTarget
SessionSyncTarget.description:
One connected-agent option in the Sessions "Sync now" dropdown.

A project host the user can target a sync at, projected to the
human-readable shape the FE renders — the grouped agent picker shows
``name`` under the ``system_name`` group label. ``id`` is the equality
handle the FE passes back as ``SessionSyncInput.host_id``; it is never
rendered as visible text (AGENTS.md "Built for Humans").

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: Syncable connected agents, in host order. Grouped by system_name on the FE.

SessionSyncTargetsResponse.title: SessionSyncTargetsResponse
SessionSyncTargetsResponse.description:
Connected agents a project can sync sessions from.

Powers the Sessions "Sync now" dropdown. Sourced from the same host
list the sync iterates server-side, projected to human display values
and grouped FE-side by ``system_name``. Empty list (never null) for a
project with no connected agents.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Distinct tag values across all sessions in the project, sorted alphabetically. Empty when no sessions in the project carry any tags.

SessionTagVocabularyResponse.title: SessionTagVocabularyResponse
SessionTagVocabularyResponse.description:
Distinct, sorted tag vocabulary for a project (DEV-743 / M8-30).

Returned by ``GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/tag-vocabulary``.
Source of truth is the union of every session's ``tags`` JSONB array —
there is no separate vocabulary table. The list is sorted
alphabetically (case-sensitive — Postgres default collation) so the
Tag popover renders a stable order without re-sorting client-side.

add.title: Add
add.description: Tags to add. Already-present tags are no-ops.

remove.title: Remove
remove.description: Tags to remove. Absent tags are no-ops.

SessionTagsUpdate.title: SessionTagsUpdate
SessionTagsUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /sessions/{id}/tags``.

Both fields are optional and default to empty lists, so a client can
add only, remove only, or no-op (empty body). The endpoint is
idempotent: adding a tag that already exists or removing one that
isn't present is silently accepted.

idx.title: Idx
idx.description: Zero-based turn index within the session.

reasoning_text.title: Reasoning Text
reasoning_text.description: Extracted reasoning trace (DEV-1018). Populated when the underlying assistant turn carried a ``<thinking>...</thinking>`` block; ``null`` everywhere else. The FE renders this in a collapsed ``<ReasoningTrace>`` disclosure component inside the assistant message bubble — never inline with the prose.

response_latency_ms.title: Response Latency Ms
response_latency_ms.description: How long the agent took to respond, in whole milliseconds — ``this_turn.created_at − previous_turn.created_at``. Populated only on assistant turns; ``null`` on the first turn, on non-assistant turns, and when the delta is non-positive (e.g. batched-trace turns sharing a timestamp). The FE renders this as an inline 'responded in X' badge and hides it when null.

agent_steps.title: Agent Steps
agent_steps.description: Ordered agent execution steps that produced this turn, sorted by ``ord`` ASC. Empty list (never null) when the turn has no recorded steps.

SessionTurnResponse.title: SessionTurnResponse
SessionTurnResponse.description:
A single conversation turn (M2-05 / DEV-653).

Mirrors a row in the ``session_turns`` table (M2-01 / DEV-635). Each
turn carries an ordered ``agent_steps`` array sourced from the
``session_agent_steps`` table (M2-02 / DEV-637) and wired into this
response by DEV-733. The list is empty when the underlying turn has
no steps — never ``null`` — so the FE renderer can render an explicit
"No agent steps" empty state without nullish-coalescing.

start_date.title: Start Date
start_date.description: Inclusive lower bound on session timestamp.

end_date.title: End Date
end_date.description: Inclusive upper bound on session timestamp.

routed_to.title: Routed To
routed_to.description: Match sessions whose agent steps include any of these route names (OR within the dimension). Empty list is a no-op.

tools_called.title: Tools Called
tools_called.description: Match sessions whose agent steps include any of these tool names (OR within the dimension). Empty list is a no-op.

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: Match sessions tagged with any of these tag values (OR within the dimension). Empty list is a no-op.

SessionsFilters.title: SessionsFilters
SessionsFilters.description:
Narrowed filter object that drives the Sessions list query.

DEV-746. The same shape is used by both ``GET /sessions`` (read)
and bulk-ops Mode B request bodies. The repository's
``build_session_filter_clauses`` helper builds the SQL WHERE
clauses from this model; new filters MUST be added in both places
or the read view and the bulk-ops view will drift.

DEV-752 (M8-39): the multi-dimension filter popover threads
``routed_to``, ``tools_called``, and ``tag`` lists through here.
Within a single dimension the values OR (any-match); across
dimensions the dimensions AND. Empty / unset lists are no-ops.

excluded_ids.title: Excluded Ids
excluded_ids.description: Row IDs to subtract from the filter set (the user de-selected these after promoting to all-matching).

SessionsSelectionByFilter.title: SessionsSelectionByFilter
SessionsSelectionByFilter.description: Sessions Mode B: ``SessionsFilters`` + an excluded_ids list.

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: Row IDs to operate on. Empty list is a no-op.

SessionsSelectionByIds.title: SessionsSelectionByIds
SessionsSelectionByIds.description: Sessions Mode A: an explicit list of session IDs.

SetRowAnnotationInput.title: SetRowAnnotationInput
SetRowAnnotationInput.description:
Body for ``PUT /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows/{row_id}/annotations/{column_key}``.

``value`` is intentionally typed as ``Any`` because the legal shape
depends on the column's ``type``:

* ``pass_fail`` -> ``bool``
* ``score``     -> ``int`` / ``float`` (inside the column's range + step)
* ``axial``     -> ``str`` (one of the column's ``config.options``)
* ``multi_tag`` -> ``list[str]`` (subset of options, unless ``allow_freeform``)
* ``comment``   -> ``str``

The service rejects mismatched shapes with a structured 400.

SetSessionAnnotationValueInput.title: SetSessionAnnotationValueInput
SetSessionAnnotationValueInput.description:
Body for ``PUT /v1/sessions/{session_id}/annotations/{column_key}``.

``value`` is intentionally typed as ``Any`` because the legal shape
depends on the column's ``type``:

* ``pass_fail`` -> ``bool``
* ``score``     -> ``int`` / ``float`` (inside the column's range)
* ``axial``     -> ``str`` (one of the column's ``config.options``)
* ``multi_tag`` -> ``list[str]`` (subset of options, unless ``allow_freeform``)
* ``comment``   -> ``str``

``null`` clears the cell. The service rejects mismatched shapes with a
structured 422.

has_sessions.title: Has Sessions
has_sessions.description: Project has at least one imported session

has_datasets.title: Has Datasets
has_datasets.description: Project has at least one dataset

has_evaluators.title: Has Evaluators
has_evaluators.description: Project has at least one evaluator

has_evaluation_runs.title: Has Evaluation Runs
has_evaluation_runs.description: Project has at least one evaluation run

SetupStatus.title: SetupStatus
SetupStatus.description:
First-run checklist completion flags for a project.

Powers the `Home` checklist that guides new projects through setup.
Each flag is true when at least one record of that type exists for
the project. `has_hosts` is not included here because hosts live in
the separate gateway service; the frontend checks it via a direct
call to the gateway hosts API.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: ID of the live source Session this row was materialised from.

display_label.title: Display Label
display_label.description: Human-readable source-session label — origin-aware: ``RUN-000123`` for a generated session, ``SES-000123`` for an external one — never the raw UUID. Mirrors ``SessionResponse.display_label``.

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: First user-role turn's text content from the source session — the Dataset Detail 'Transcript' column preview. ``null`` when the session has no user turn.

turn_count.title: Turn Count
turn_count.description: Total turn count of the source session ('Turns' column).

routed_to.title: Routed To
routed_to.description: Distinct ``route``-kind agent-step names for the source session, sorted alphabetically ('Routed To' column). Empty list (never null) when the session recorded no routing steps. Mirrors ``SessionResponse.routed_to``.

tools_called.title: Tools Called
tools_called.description: Distinct ``tool``-kind agent-step names for the source session, sorted alphabetically ('Tools Called' column). Empty list (never null) when the session called no tools. Mirrors ``SessionResponse.tools_called``.

date.title: Date
date.description: Source session timestamp ('Date' column). ``null`` only when the session row carries no timestamp.

user.title: User
user.description: External end-user id of the source session ('User' column) — ``Session.end_user_id``. ``null`` for sessions whose source system carries no end-user identifier.

duration_ms.title: Duration Ms
duration_ms.description: Source session duration in milliseconds ('Duration' column), derived as the span between the first and last session turn's ``created_at``. ``null`` for a session with fewer than two turns (no measurable span).

SourceSessionProjection.title: SourceSessionProjection
SourceSessionProjection.description:
Per-row projection of the live source Session for a materialised row.

A DatasetRow materialised from a session (``origin_kind ==
'session'``) carries an ``origin_session_id`` FK to the live
``Session`` it was copied from. The Dataset Detail rows table's
prototype column model is *session-shaped* — its ``SESSION_COLUMNS``
(``docs/design/_source/columns.jsx:5-16``) surface a transcript
preview, routed-to topics, tools called, turn count, the session
date, the originating user, and a duration. None of that lives on
the DatasetRow itself (an Example is an immutable *copy* of
conversation turns, not a live reference), so this block is a
read-time projection of the source Session.

It is attached to :class:`DatasetRowResponse.source_session` only
for rows whose source session still exists. Rows materialised from
an upload — or rows whose source session has since been deleted
(``origin_session_id`` is ``ON DELETE SET NULL``) — carry
``source_session = null``, so the FE only paints the session-shaped
columns for rows that genuinely back them (AGENTS.md "Built for
Humans and Agents": absent fields must not surface zero-value
placeholders).

teams.title: Teams
teams.description: Every team in the workspace, each with its member count.

TeamListResponse.title: TeamListResponse
TeamListResponse.description: The **Teams** tab payload — every team in the caller's workspace with its member count.

id.title: Id
id.description: The member's email (the subject id; membership is keyed on lower(email)).

TeamMemberRef.title: TeamMemberRef
TeamMemberRef.description:
A single team member — a typed subject (``kind`` + ``id``), never a bare string.

``kind`` is ``"user"`` and ``id`` is the person's email (the engine/strategy key membership on
``lower(email)``). Used both as the wire form of a resolver :class:`~trustai_core.authz.Subject`
on reads and as the add-member request body.

team_id.title: Team Id
team_id.description: The team these members belong to.

members.title: Members
members.description: Every member of the team (typed subjects).

TeamMembersResponse.title: TeamMembersResponse
TeamMembersResponse.description: The team detail sheet's member list — the principals in a team's member-set.

name.title: Name
name.description: The team's display name now in effect.

TeamResponse.title: TeamResponse
TeamResponse.description: Echo of a created/renamed team — its id + name now in effect (idempotent).

name.title: Name
name.description: The team's display name (or the missing-name marker if absent).

member_count.title: Member Count
member_count.description: The number of members in the team.

name_missing.title: Name Missing
name_missing.description: True when the team's display name could not be resolved (name is the marker).

TeamSummary.title: TeamSummary
TeamSummary.description:
One row of the **Teams** tab: a team + its member count (the list-with-counts surface).

``member_count`` is the resolved size of the team's member-set; ``name_missing`` is True when the
team's display name could not be resolved (the ``name`` then degrades to the package's missing-name
marker) so the UI can show a placeholder rather than the raw marker.

first_user_turn.title: First User Turn
first_user_turn.description: The conversation's opening user turn content, taken from the snapshot's first ``role == 'user'`` turn. ``None`` when no user turn is recorded.

turn_count.title: Turn Count
turn_count.description: Total turn count for the conversation (every role). The prototype renders ``+N more turns`` where ``N = turn_count - 1`` when a first user turn was found, else just ``turn_count`` (so a single-turn assistant-only row reads as '+1 more turn' against a '—' first-user fallback).

TranscriptPreviewProjection.title: TranscriptPreviewProjection
TranscriptPreviewProjection.description:
First-user-turn preview + total turn count for one evaluation result.

The evaluation-run-detail matrix's Transcript column is the
surface's "star" cell — every row paints the conversation's opening
user turn over "+N more turns" so a reviewer can scan a hundred
examples without opening the side panel. The prototype pins the
shape at ``docs/design/_source/columns.jsx:155-175`` (the
``case "preview"`` renderer in ``table.jsx``): ``firstUser.content``
over ``+{remaining} more turns`` where ``remaining = turnCount -
(firstUser ? 1 : 0)``.

The matrix list endpoint projects this per result so the FE never
has to re-walk the snapshot blob on the client. Service-layer
derivation prefers ``example_snapshot.turns`` (the frozen
conversation captured at run-dequeue time per RFC 0003 §4.1) and
falls back to live ``example_turns`` only for legacy / dev-seed
rows whose snapshot pre-dates turn capture — the same fallback the
Input / Expected projections use.

``first_user_turn`` is ``None`` when the snapshot's ``turns[]``
has no entry with ``role == "user"`` (a result with only assistant
turns, or no turns at all). The FE renders the row's empty-cell
fallback ("—") in that case.

TrendPoint.title: TrendPoint
TrendPoint.description:
One historical pass-rate datapoint for the evaluator trend sparkline.

A point is anchored to a specific ``EvaluationRun`` so the FE can
align the sparkline against the runs list (deep-link a tooltip to
the matching run detail). ``pass_rate`` is the mean of
``EvaluationScore.verdict == "pass"`` across every result row in
that run that scored the evaluator — ``None`` when the run produced
no scores for this evaluator (in-flight, errored before scoring,
etc.) so the sparkline can render a gap instead of a misleading
zero.

score.title: Score
score.description: Trust score 0-100 based on pass rate

failed_runs.title: Failed Runs
failed_runs.description: Runs with at least one failure

TrustScoreSummary.title: TrustScoreSummary
TrustScoreSummary.description: Overall trust score derived from test run pass rates.

UpdateAgentVersionInput.title: UpdateAgentVersionInput
UpdateAgentVersionInput.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /agent-versions/{id}``.

Only the label is mutable. Re-parenting to a different project is
intentionally not supported — agent versions belong to the project
they were created in.

UpdateBudgetInput.title: UpdateBudgetInput
UpdateBudgetInput.description: Input for updating a project's budget cap.

UpdateDatasetColumnInput.title: UpdateDatasetColumnInput
UpdateDatasetColumnInput.description:
Body for ``PATCH /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns/{column_key}``.

``key`` and ``type`` are immutable on update — once a column has
annotation values written against it, renaming the key or changing
the type would silently invalidate every existing value. Callers
that need a different type must delete + create.

UpdateDatasetRowInput.title: UpdateDatasetRowInput
UpdateDatasetRowInput.description:
Body for ``PATCH /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows/{row_id}``.

Post-M3-11 the row body is metadata only — turn content is edited
through the ``ExampleTurn`` endpoints. This shape covers
``source_session_id`` and ``tags``; everything else lives on
children.

``input_data`` / ``expected_output`` are still accepted on the wire
so the polymorphic Paddington update path doesn't break for clients
that learned the old shape, but they are silently ignored —
callers MUST go through the turn endpoints to mutate conversation
body.

UpdateDatasetTagVocabularyInput.title: UpdateDatasetTagVocabularyInput
UpdateDatasetTagVocabularyInput.description:
Body for ``PUT /datasets/{dataset_id}/tag-vocabulary``.

``tags`` replaces the dataset's stored tag vocabulary wholesale.
Each tag must be a non-empty string; duplicates (case-insensitive)
are rejected so the vocabulary stays a set. Whitespace is trimmed at
the service layer before persisting.

Removing a tag from the vocabulary does NOT strip it off rows that
already carry it — row tags are edited through the row endpoints.
The read endpoint folds those still-in-use tags back in, so a
removed-but-used tag reappears until every row drops it.

UpdateExampleTurnInput.title: UpdateExampleTurnInput
UpdateExampleTurnInput.description:
Patch a turn's content and/or role. ``idx`` is fixed by ordering
operations (insert / delete) and intentionally not patchable here —
callers should delete + insert if they need to reorder.

UpdateIoSplitInput.title: UpdateIoSplitInput
UpdateIoSplitInput.description:
PATCH ioSplit on a DatasetRow.

``io_split`` is a single integer in ``[0, len(turns)]`` — it's the
boundary index that separates "input" turns from the "expected
output" turn(s). The service validates the upper bound against the
row's current turn count.

workspace_role.title: Workspace Role
workspace_role.description: The new workspace role (admin | member | guest).

UpdateMemberRoleInput.title: UpdateMemberRoleInput
UpdateMemberRoleInput.description: Body for a member role change. ``owner`` is rejected (422) — ownership is not role-assignable.

UpdatePersonaInput.title: UpdatePersonaInput
UpdatePersonaInput.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /v1/personas/{id}``.

Every field is optional; only the supplied keys are updated. Re-parenting
to a different project is intentionally not supported — personas belong
to the project they were created in.

agent_platform.title: Agent Platform
agent_platform.description: Primary agent platform this project will evaluate

emit_paddington_sessions.title: Emit Paddington Sessions
emit_paddington_sessions.description: Whether to capture in-app TrustAI agent (Paddington) conversations as evaluation sessions for this project.

UpdateScenarioInput.title: UpdateScenarioInput
UpdateScenarioInput.description:
Body for ``PATCH /scenarios/{scenario_id}``.

The project-scoped, tenant-scoped write side of the flat Scenario
facade. It persists the inline edits the ``/scenarios`` table fires
onto the re-rooted ``dataset_rows`` storage, addressed by the
scenario id directly — no O(datasets) probe to discover the owning
dataset (the read shape already surfaces ``dataset_id`` for callers
that prefer the dataset-row write path).

Every field is optional and uses unset-means-leave-unchanged
semantics. An explicit empty string for ``name`` clears the label.

UpdateSessionAnnotationColumnInput.title: UpdateSessionAnnotationColumnInput
UpdateSessionAnnotationColumnInput.description:
Body for ``PATCH .../sessions/annotation-columns/{column_key}``.

``key`` and ``type`` are immutable on update — once values are written
against a column, renaming the key or changing the type would silently
invalidate every existing value. Callers needing a different type
delete + create.

capture_trustai_agent_sessions.title: Capture Trustai Agent Sessions
capture_trustai_agent_sessions.description: Whether to capture in-app TrustAI agent conversations. Toggling on creates an auto-managed project; toggling off deletes that project and all its captured data.

UpdateWorkspaceSettingsInput.title: UpdateWorkspaceSettingsInput
UpdateWorkspaceSettingsInput.description: PATCH body. Today the only mutable field is the capture toggle.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: One entry per registered provider, in stable slug-alphabetic order

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.default: Cost values are Bifrost's per-call estimates and may differ from the provider's invoice. Use them for relative spend comparison, not billing reconciliation.

success_rate.title: Success Rate
success_rate.description: Fraction 0-100 of calls that returned a 2xx upstream response

average_latency_ms.title: Average Latency Ms
average_latency_ms.description: Mean upstream round-trip latency in milliseconds

total_cost_usd.title: Total Cost Usd
total_cost_usd.description: Bifrost-estimated cost in USD using its pricing datasheet. Directional — NOT authoritative billing; provider invoices remain the source of truth.

UsageWindow.title: UsageWindow
UsageWindow.description: Aggregate totals over a single time window for one provider.

UserDisplay.title: UserDisplay
UserDisplay.description:
A WorkOS user projected down to display-relevant fields.

Mirrors the subset of the WorkOS ``User`` record (see
``services/api/src/core/workos.py`` ``serialize_user``) that the
frontend needs to render an author label. WorkOS is the source of
truth — there is no local ``users`` table.

WorkspaceCaptureImpactResponse.title: WorkspaceCaptureImpactResponse
WorkspaceCaptureImpactResponse.description:
Summary used by the confirmation modal when toggling capture OFF.

Counts data that would be removed if the user confirms. The modal
surfaces these to the user so they can make an informed choice
rather than discovering loss after the fact.

member_id.title: Member Id
member_id.description: The member's stable WorkOS user id.

name.title: Name
name.description: The member's display name (first + last); empty if unknown.

workspace_role.title: Workspace Role
workspace_role.description: The member's workspace role slug on this org.

teams.title: Teams
teams.description: The teams the member belongs to (the Members table's team chips).

joined.title: Joined
joined.description: When the member joined the workspace.

WorkspaceMemberDetail.title: WorkspaceMemberDetail
WorkspaceMemberDetail.description: One assembled Members-table row: a member's identity + role + team chips + tenure.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next member page; null at the end.

WorkspaceMembersResponse.title: WorkspaceMembersResponse
WorkspaceMembersResponse.description:
The assembled Members tab payload: a page of members (with team chips) + pending invites.

Members and pending invites are distinct lists — a pending invite is not yet a member, so it is
never folded into a member row. ``next_cursor`` paginates the MEMBER list (WorkOS-cursor-based).

WorkspaceSettingsResponse.title: WorkspaceSettingsResponse
WorkspaceSettingsResponse.description:
The full workspace settings shape surfaced to the frontend.

``trustai_agent_sessions_project_id`` is the auto-managed project's
UUID when capture is on, otherwise ``None``. The frontend deep-links
into that project's Sessions list so users have one click to "see
the captured conversations" right next to the toggle.
